items.title: Content Caching
items.summary: The payload that configures the Content Caching service.
items.searchKeys: PublicRanges RangesItem last first type Port PeerLocalSubnetsOnly PeerListenRanges PeerFilterRanges ParentSelectionPolicy Parents ParentsItem LogClientIdentity LocalSubnetsOnly ListenWithPeersAndParents ListenRangesOnly ListenRanges KeepAwake DisplayAlerts DenyTetheredCaching DataPath CacheLimit AutoEnableTetheredCaching AutoActivation AllowSharedCaching AllowPersonalCaching AllowCacheDelete
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures the Content Caching service. An array of dictionaries describing a range of public IP addresses that the cloud servers should use for matching clients to content caches. A range of IP addresses to cache. The last IP address in the range. The first IP address in the range. The IP address type. The TCP port number on which the content cache accepts requests for uploads or downloads. Set to `0` to pick a random, available port. If `true`, the content cache only peers with other content caches on the same immediate local network, rather than with content caches that use the same public IP address as the device. When `PeerLocalSubnetsOnly` is `true`, it overrides the configuration of `PeerFilterRanges` and `PeerListenRanges`. If the network changes, the local network peering restrictions update appropriately. If `false`, the content cache defers to `PeerFilterRanges` and `PeerListenRanges` for configuring the peering restrictions. An array of dictionaries describing a range of peer IP addresses the content cache responds to. When `PeerListenRanges` is an empty array, the content cache responds with an error to all cache queries. An array of dictionaries describing a range of peer IP addresses that the content cache uses to filter its list of peers to query for content. The content cache only queries peers in `PeerFilterRanges`. When `PeerFilterRanges` is an empty array, the content cache doesn't query any peers. The policy to implement when choosing among more than one configured parent content cache. With every policy, the system skips parent caches that are temporarily unavailable. Allowed values:

- `first-available`: Always use the first available parent in the Parents list. Use this policy to designate permanent primary, secondary, and subsequent parents.
- `url-path-hash`: Hash the path part of the requested URL so that the same parent is always used for the same URL. This is useful for maximizing the size of the combined caches of     the parents.
- `random`: Choose a parent at random. Use this policy for load balancing.
- `round-robin`: Rotate through the parents in order. Use this policy for load balancing.
- `sticky-available`: Use the first available parent in the Parents list until it becomes unavailable, then advance to the next one. Use this policy for designating floating primary, secondary, and subsequent parents. An array of the local IP addresses of other content caches that this cache should download from or upload to, instead of downloading from or uploading to Apple directly. The system ignores invalid addresses and addresses of computers that aren't content caches. The system skips Parent caches that become unavailable. If all parent content caches become unavailable, the content cache downloads from or uploads to Apple directly, until a parent content cache becomes available again. An IP address. If `true`, the Content Cache logs the IP address and port number of the clients that request content. If `true`, the content cache offers content to clients only on the same immediate local network only. No content is offered to clients on other networks reachable by the content cache. If `LocalSubnetsOnly` is `true`, the system ignores `ListenRanges`. If `true`, the content cache provides content to the clients in the union of the `ListenRanges`, `PeerListenRanges` and `Parents`. If `true`, the content cache provides content to the clients in the `ListenRanges`. An array of dictionaries that describe a range of client IP addresses to serve. If `true`, the system prevents the computer from sleeping as long as Content Caching is on (System Preferences > Sharing > Content Caching is on). Customers who want Content Caching to be as available as much as possible should turn this setting on. Available in macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, Content Caching displays exceptional conditions (alerts) as system notifications in the upper corner of the screen. Alerts were automatically displayed starting in macOS 10.13. In macOS 10.15 the alerts are off by default, but still available through this setting. Available in macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, the system disables tethered caching. The path to the directory used to store cached content. Changing this setting manually doesn't automatically move cached content from the old location to the new one. To move content automatically, use the Sharing preference's Content Caching pane. The value must be (or end with) `/Library/Application Support/Apple/AssetCache/Data`.

The system creates a directory and its intermediates for the given data path if it doesn't already exist. The directory is owned by `_assetcache:_assetcache` and has mode 0750. Its immediate parent directory (`.../Library/Application Support/Apple/AssetCache`) is owned by `_assetcache:_assetcache` and has mode `0755`. The maximum number of bytes of disk space to use for the content cache. Set to `0` for unlimited disk space. If `true`, the system automatically enables Internet connection sharing when possible and prevent disabling Internet connection sharing. `DenyTetheredCaching` overrides `AutoEnableTetheredCaching`. Tethered caching requires Content Caching.

Available in macOS 10.15.4 and later. If `true`, the system automatically activates the content cache when possible and prevents disabling it. If `allowContentCaching` is `false`, `AutoActivation` is also `false`.

Removing a profile that set `AutoActivation` to `true` doesn't deactivate the Content Cache. If `true`, the system caches non-iCloud content, such as apps and software updates. Changes to this value don't have an immediate effect. Clients may take some time, such as hours or days, to react to changes.

> Note:
> At least one of the `AllowPersonalCaching` or `AllowSharedCaching` keys need to be `true`. If `true`, the system caches the user's iCloud data. Changes to this value don't have an immediate effect. Clients may take some time, such as hours or days, to react to changes.

> Note:
> At least one of the `AllowPersonalCaching` or `AllowSharedCaching` keys need to be `true`. If true, the system purges content from the cache automatically when it needs disk space for other apps when free disk space runs low on the computer. Set to `false` to maximize effectiveness of Content Caching. Available in macOS 10.15 and later. Configures the Content Caching service.

declaration.description: The payload that configures the Content Caching service.

payload.content: Configures the Content Caching service.

payloadkeys.content: If true, the system purges content from the cache automatically when it needs disk space for other apps when free disk space runs low on the computer. Set to `false` to maximize effectiveness of Content Caching. Available in macOS 10.15 and later.

If `true`, the system caches the user's iCloud data. Changes to this value don't have an immediate effect. Clients may take some time, such as hours or days, to react to changes.

> Note:
> At least one of the `AllowPersonalCaching` or `AllowSharedCaching` keys need to be `true`.

> Note:
> At least one of the `AllowPersonalCaching` or `AllowSharedCaching` keys need to be `true`.

If `true`, the system automatically activates the content cache when possible and prevents disabling it. If `allowContentCaching` is `false`, `AutoActivation` is also `false`.

Removing a profile that set `AutoActivation` to `true` doesn't deactivate the Content Cache.

If `true`, the system automatically enables Internet connection sharing when possible and prevent disabling Internet connection sharing. `DenyTetheredCaching` overrides `AutoEnableTetheredCaching`. Tethered caching requires Content Caching.

Available in macOS 10.15.4 and later.
The maximum number of bytes of disk space to use for the content cache. Set to `0` for unlimited disk space.

To move content automatically, use the Sharing preference's Content Caching pane. The value must be (or end with) `/Library/Application Support/Apple/AssetCache/Data`.

The system creates a directory and its intermediates for the given data path if it doesn't already exist. The directory is owned by `_assetcache:_assetcache` and has mode 0750. Its immediate parent directory (`.../Library/Application Support/Apple/AssetCache`) is owned by `_assetcache:_assetcache` and has mode `0755`.
If `true`, the system disables tethered caching.

If `true`, Content Caching displays exceptional conditions (alerts) as system notifications in the upper corner of the screen. Alerts were automatically displayed starting in macOS 10.13. In macOS 10.15 the alerts are off by default, but still available through this setting. Available in macOS 10.15 and later.

If `true`, the system prevents the computer from sleeping as long as Content Caching is on (System Preferences > Sharing > Content Caching is on). Customers who want Content Caching to be as available as much as possible should turn this setting on. Available in macOS 10.15 and later.

items.searchContent: The payload that configures an Active Directory (AD) domain. The number of days before requiring a change of the computer trust account password. Set to `0` to disable the feature. If `true`, the system enables the `ADTrustChangePassIntervalDays` key. An array of strings that represent the interfaces allowed for dynamic DNS updates, such as en0 and en1. If `true`, the system enables the `ADRestrictDDNS` key. The packet encryption policy. If `true`, the system enables the `ADPacketEncrypt` key. The packet signing policy. If `true`, the system enables the `ADPacketSign` key. The primary user account naming convention; either `forest` or `domain`. If `true`, the system enables the `ADNamespace` key. The list of Active Directory groups with admin access. If `true`, the system enables the `ADDomainAdminGroupList` key. The preferred domain server. If `true`, the system enables the `ADPreferredDCServer` key. The map group GID to attribute. If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapGGIDAttributeFlag` key. The map GID to attribute. If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapGIDAttribute` key. The map UID to attribute. If `true`, the system enables the `ADMapUIDAttribute` key. The default user shell. If `true`, the system enables the `ADDefaultUserShell` key. If `true`, the system allows authentication from any domain in the namespace. If `true`, the system enables the `ADAllowMultiDomainAuth` key. If `true`, the system uses the UNC path from Active Directory to derive the network home location. If `true`, the system enables the `ADUseWindowsUNCPath` key. If `true`, the system forces a local home directory. If `true`, the system enables the `ADForceHomeLocal` key. If `true`, the system enables the warning before creating the mobile account. If `true`, the system enables the `ADWarnUserBeforeCreatingMA` key. If `true`, the system creates a mobile account at login. If `true`, the system enables the `ADCreateMobileAccountAtLogin` key. The network home protocol to use: `afp` or `smb`. The organizational unit to add the joining computer object to. The directory service description. The client's identifier. The password of the account for the domain. The user name of the account for the domain. The Active Directory domain to join. In macOS 10.9 and later, a configuration profile can be used to configure macOS to join an Active Directory (AD) domain. Advanced AD options available via Directory Utility or the dsconfigad command line tool can also be set using a configuration profile.

items.title: Media Management: Disc Burning
items.summary: The payload that configures disc-burning settings.
items.searchKeys: BurnSupport
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures disc-burning settings. Configure disc-burn. Allowed values:
- `off`: The system disables disc burning.
- `on`: The system allows normal default operation. Setting this key to `on` doesn't enable disc burn support if other mechanisms or preferences disabled it. Needs to be enabled with the `Finder` profile.
- `authenticate`: The system requires authentication.

declaration.title: Media Management: Disc Burning
declaration.description: The payload that configures disc-burning settings.

payloadkeys.content:
Configure disc-burn. Allowed values:
- `off`: The system disables disc burning.
- `on`: The system allows normal default operation. Setting this key to `on` doesn't enable disc burn support if other mechanisms or preferences disabled it. Needs to be enabled with the `Finder` profile.
- `authenticate`: The system requires authentication.

items.title: Mobile Accounts
items.summary: The payload that configures mobile accounts on the device.
items.searchKeys: cachedaccounts.askForSecureTokenAuthBypass cachedaccounts.expiry.delete.disusedSeconds cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate.allowNever com.apple.cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate com.apple.cachedaccounts.CreateAtLogin
items.searchContent: The payload that configures mobile accounts on the device. If `true`, the system bypasses the secure token authorization dialog. This dialog only appears on APFS volumes. The minimum number of seconds a mobile account can exist before the system makes an automatic attempt to remove the mobile account. Set to `0` to attempt removing it at the next login or logout. Set to `-1` to never attempt removing the mobile account. If `true`, the system allows the user to stop the prompts about mobile account creation every time the user logs in. This key is only valid if `com.apple.cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate` is `true`. If `true`, the system asks the user whether to create the mobile account and it allows the user to not create it. If `true`, the system creates the mobile account at login time. Sets up mobile account options for network based user accounts.

declaration.title: Mobile Accounts
declaration.description: The payload that configures mobile accounts on the device.

payload.content: Sets up mobile account options for network based user accounts.

payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system creates the mobile account at login time.
If `true`, the system asks the user whether to create the mobile account and it allows the user to not create it.
If `true`, the system allows the user to stop the prompts about mobile account creation every time the user logs in. This key is only valid if `com.apple.cachedaccounts.WarnOnCreate` is `true`.

The minimum number of seconds a mobile account can exist before the system makes an automatic attempt to remove the mobile account. Set to `0` to attempt removing it at the next login or logout. Set to `-1` to never attempt removing the mobile account.
If `true`, the system bypasses the secure token authorization dialog. This dialog only appears on APFS volumes.

items.title: Time Server
items.summary: The payload that configures the time server.
items.searchKeys: timeZone timeServer
items.searchContent: The payload that configures the time server. If multiple profiles with this payload are sent, the system sets the device's time server to the value in the last payload installed. Removing the payload won't change the settings back to the prior settings. The time zone path location string in `/usr/share/zoneinfo/`; for example, `America/Denver` or `Zulu`. The NTP server to connect to. In macOS 10.13 and later, only one time server is supported. Settings for time zone and server. If multiple profiles with this payload are sent, the device's time server will be set to the value in the last payload installed. Removing the payload will not change the settings back to the prior settings.

declaration.title: Time Server
declaration.description: The payload that configures the time server.

payload.content: Settings for time zone and server. If multiple profiles with this payload are sent, the device's time server will be set to the value in the last payload installed. Removing the payload will not change the settings back to the prior settings.

payloadkeys.content: The NTP server to connect to. In macOS 10.13 and later, only one time server is supported.
The time zone path location string in `/usr/share/zoneinfo/`; for example, `America/Denver` or `Zulu`.

notes.content: If multiple profiles with this payload are sent, the system sets the device's time server to the value in the last payload installed. Removing the payload won't change the settings back to the prior settings.

items.title: FDE FileVault
items.summary: The payload that configures FileVault.
items.searchKeys: ForceEnableInSetupAssistant DeferDontAskAtUserLogout DeferForceAtUserLoginMaxBypassAttempts UseKeychain Password Username PayloadCertificateUUID Certificate OutputPath ShowRecoveryKey UseRecoveryKey UserEntersMissingInfo Defer Enable
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures FileVault. FileVault 2 performs full XTS-AES 128 encryption on the contents of a volume. Removing the FileVault payload doesn't disable FileVault.

As of macOS 10.15, FileVault settings require supervision or user approval when installed manually. If `true`, and installation of this payload occurs after enrolling with MDM in Setup Assistant, the system requests Setup Assistant to enable FileVault at setup time.

To use this, enable the Await Device Configured DEP configuration option and send this profile with this key set, before sending the `DeviceConfiguredCommand`.

An admin SecureToken user is required, otherwise the FileVault pane does not appear. If `true`, the system prevents requests to enable FileVault at user logout time. The maximum number of times users can bypass enabling FileVault before the system requires the user to enable it to log in. If the value is `0`, the system requires the user to enable FileVault the next time they attempt to log in. Set this key to `-1` to disable this feature. If `true` and you don't include certificate information in this payload, the system uses the keychain created at `/Library/Keychains/FileVaultMaster.keychain` when it adds the institutional recovery key. The password of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault. Use the `UserEntersMissingInfo` key to prompt for this information. The user name of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault. The UUID of the payload within the same profile containing the asymmetric recovery key certificate payload. The DER-encoded certificate data if the system creates an institutional recovery key. This key isn't supported on a Mac with Apple silicon. The path to the location of the recovery key and computer information property list. If `false`, the system prevents display of the personal recovery key to the user after the system enables FileVault. If `true`, the system creates a personal recovery key and displays it to the user. If `true`, the system enables a prompt for missing user name or password fields. If `true`, the system defers enabling FileVault until the designated user logs out. For details, see `fdesetup(8)`. Only a local user or a mobile account user can enable FileVault. Set to `On` to enable FileVault and set to `Off` to disable FileVault. Payloads set to `On` sent through MDM need to either include full authentication information in the payload or have the `Defer` option set to `true`. When `Defer` is `true`, the system prompts for the authentication information when the user enables FileVault. The FileVault payload only works on macOS to enable or disable FileVault. Starting with macOS 10.15, this payload requires UAMDM to enable FileVault.

declaration.title: FDE FileVault
declaration.description: The payload that configures FileVault.

payload.content: The FileVault payload only works on macOS to enable or disable FileVault. Starting with macOS 10.15, this payload requires UAMDM to enable FileVault.

payloadkeys.content: Set to `On` to enable FileVault and set to `Off` to disable FileVault. Payloads set to `On` sent through MDM need to either include full authentication information in the payload or have the `Defer` option set to `true`. When `Defer` is `true`, the system prompts for the authentication information when the user enables FileVault.
If `true`, the system defers enabling FileVault until the designated user logs out. For details, see `fdesetup(8)`. Only a local user or a mobile account user can enable FileVault.
If `true`, the system enables a prompt for missing user name or password fields.
If `true`, the system creates a personal recovery key and displays it to the user.
If `false`, the system prevents display of the personal recovery key to the user after the system enables FileVault.
The path to the location of the recovery key and computer information property list.
The DER-encoded certificate data if the system creates an institutional recovery key. This key isn't supported on a Mac with Apple silicon.
The UUID of the payload within the same profile containing the asymmetric recovery key certificate payload.
The user name of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault.
The password of the Open Directory user to add to FileVault. Use the `UserEntersMissingInfo` key to prompt for this information.

If `true` and you don't include certificate information in this payload, the system uses the keychain created at `/Library/Keychains/FileVaultMaster.keychain` when it adds the institutional recovery key.

The maximum number of times users can bypass enabling FileVault before the system requires the user to enable it to log in. If the value is `0`, the system requires the user to enable FileVault the next time they attempt to log in. Set this key to `-1` to disable this feature.
If `true`, the system prevents requests to enable FileVault at user logout time.

If `true`, and installation of this payload occurs after enrolling with MDM in Setup Assistant, the system requests Setup Assistant to enable FileVault at setup time.

To use this, enable the Await Device Configured DEP configuration option and send this profile with this key set, before sending the `DeviceConfiguredCommand`.

An admin SecureToken user is required, otherwise the FileVault pane does not appear.

notes.content:
FileVault 2 performs full XTS-AES 128 encryption on the contents of a volume. Removing the FileVault payload doesn't disable FileVault.

As of macOS 10.15, FileVault settings require supervision or user approval when installed manually.

items.title: NSExtension Management
items.summary: The payload that configures the extensions that the system allows or disallows to run on the device.
items.searchKeys: DeniedExtensionPoints DeniedExtensionPointsItem DeniedExtensions DeniedExtensionsItem AllowedExtensions AllowedExtensionsItem
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures the extensions that the system allows or disallows to run on the device. You can manage extensions by bundle identifiers in allow and deny lists, or by a deny list of extension points.

You can also start with all public extensions disallowed. To do so, include `AllPublicExtensionPoints` in `DeniedExtensionPoints`. This causes the system to expand the list to include all extensions that belong to any public extension points. This expansion occurs at evaluation time. The list of extension points can change from release to release. The expanded list disallows Apple and third-party extensions, but still allows extensions that belong to system-critcial extension points to execute. An array of extension points for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run. An extension identifier. An array of bundle identifiers for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run. An extension identifier. An array of bundle identifiers for allowed extensions. An extension identifier. Specifies which NSExtension extensions are to be allowed or disallowed on a system. Extensions can be managed by bundleID allow/deny lists and "extension points".

payloadkeys.content: An array of bundle identifiers for allowed extensions.

subkeys.content: An extension identifier.

payloadkeys.content: An array of bundle identifiers for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run.

subkeys.content: An extension identifier.

payloadkeys.content: An array of extension points for extensions that the system doesn't allow to run.

subkeys.content: An extension identifier.

notes.content:
You can manage extensions by bundle identifiers in allow and deny lists, or by a deny list of extension points.

You can also start with all public extensions disallowed. To do so, include `AllPublicExtensionPoints` in `DeniedExtensionPoints`. This causes the system to expand the list to include all extensions that belong to any public extension points. This expansion occurs at evaluation time. The list of extension points can change from release to release. The expanded list disallows Apple and third-party extensions, but still allows extensions that belong to system-critcial extension points to execute.

If `false`, deselects the "Download new updates when available from the App Store" option and prevents the user from changing the option. If `false`, restricts the "Install macOS Updates" option and prevents the user from changing the option. If `true`, restrict app installations to admin users. This key has the same function as the  `restrict-store-require-admin-to-install` key in the `com.apple.appstore` payload. If `true`, prerelease software can be installed on this computer. The URL of the software update catalog. This property is not supported in macOS 11 and later. Software update catalog options.

items.title: Privacy Preferences Policy Control

items.searchKeys: Services BluetoothAlways IdentityDict AEReceiverCodeRequirement AEReceiverIdentifierType AEReceiverIdentifier Comment Authorization Allowed StaticCode CodeRequirement IdentifierType Identifier SystemPolicyAppData SystemPolicyAppBundles SystemPolicyRemovableVolumes SystemPolicyNetworkVolumes SystemPolicyDownloadsFolder SystemPolicyDocumentsFolder SystemPolicyDesktopFolder SpeechRecognition ScreenCapture ListenEvent FileProviderPresence MediaLibrary AppleEvents SystemPolicySysAdminFiles SystemPolicyAllFiles PostEvent Accessibility Microphone Camera Photos Reminders Calendar AddressBook
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures privacy preferences. A dictionary whose keys are limited to the privacy policy control services.  In the case of conflicting specifications, the most restrictive setting (deny) is used. Specifies the policies for the app to access Bluetooth devices. A dictionary listing apps and the privacy policy to apply to them. The code requirement for the receiving binary. This code requirement is required for AppleEvents service; not valid for other services. The type of AEReceiverIdentifier value, either `bundleID` or `path`. This setting is required for AppleEvents service; not valid for other services. The identifier of the process receiving an AppleEvent sent by the Identifier process. This identifier is required for AppleEvents service; not valid for other services. Not used. The `Authorization` key is an optional replacement for the `Allowed` key, which has one of the following possible values:

- `Allow`: Equivalent to a `true` value for the `Allowed` key
- `Deny`: Equivalent to a `false` value for the `Allowed` key
- `AllowStandardUserToSetSystemService`: Allows a standard (non-admin) user to configure the permissions for the specified app in the Privacy preferences for services that otherwise require admin authorization; only valid for the `ListenEvent` and `ScreenCapture` services

> Note:
> Every payload needs to include either `Authorization` or `Allowed`, but not both.

Available in macOS 11 and later. If `true`, access is granted; otherwise, the process doesn't have access. The user isn't prompted and can't change this value.

> Note:
> Every payload needs to include either `Authorization` or `Allowed`, but not both. If `true`, statically validate the code requirement. Used only if the process invalidates its dynamic code signature. Obtained via the command `codesign -display -r -`. The type of identifier value. Application bundles must be identified by bundle ID. Nonbundled binaries must be identified by installation path. Helper tools embedded within an application bundle automatically inherit the permissions of their enclosing app bundle. The bundle ID or installation path of the binary.

> Note:
> This value is case-sensitive. Specifies the policies for the app to access the data of other apps. Allows the application to update or delete other apps. Available in macOS 13 and later. Allows the application to access files on removable volumes. Allows the application to access files on network volumes. Allows the application to access files in the user's Downloads folder. Allows the application to access files in the user's Documents folder. Allows the application to access files in the user's Desktop folder. Allows the application to use the system Speech Recognition facility and to send speech data to Apple. Allows the application to capture (read) the contents of the system display. Access to the contents can't be given in a profile; it can only be denied. Allows the application to use CoreGraphics and HID APIs to listen to (receive) CGEvents and HID events from all processes. Access to these events can't be given in a profile; it can only be denied. Allows a File Provider application to know when the user is using files managed by the File Provider. Allows the application to access Apple Music, music and video activity, and the media library. Specifies the policies for the app sending restricted AppleEvents to another process. Allows the application access to some files used in system administration. Allows the application access to all protected files, including system administration files. Specifies the policies for the application to use CoreGraphics APIs to send CGEvents to the system event stream. Specifies the policies for the app via the Accessibility subsystem. The ability to grant access by this profile is deprecated as of macOS 26.2, and will be removed in macOS 27.0. A system microphone. Access to the microphone can't be given in a profile; it can only be denied. A system camera. Access to the camera can't be given in a profile; it can only be denied. The pictures managed by the Photos app in `~/Pictures/.photoslibrary`. Specifies the policies for reminders information managed by the Reminders app. Specifies the policies for calendar information managed by the Calendar.app. Specifies the policies for contact information managed by the Contacts.app.

items.title: AirPlay Security
items.summary: The payload that configures Apple TV for a particular style of AirPlay security.
items.searchKeys: Password AccessType SecurityType
items.searchTitles: AirPlay Security Password Access Type Security Type
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures Apple TV for a particular style of AirPlay security. The AirPlay password; required if `SecurityType` is `PASSWORD`. The access policy for AirPlay.

`ANY` allows connections from both Ethernet, Wi-Fi, and Apple Wireless Direct Link.

`WIFI_ONLY` allows connections only from devices on the same Ethernet or Wi-Fi network as Apple TV. The security policy for AirPlay. Allowed values:

- `PASSCODE_ONCE`: Requires an onscreen passcode on first connection from a device. Subsequent connections from the same device aren't prompted.
- `PASSCODE_ALWAYS`: Requires an onscreen passcode for every AirPlay connection. After an AirPlay connection ends, the system allows reconnecting within 30 seconds without a password.
- `PASSWORD`: Requires the passphrase set for `Password`.

> Note:
> `NONE` was deprecated in tvOS 11.3. Existing profiles that use `NONE` get the `PASSWORD_ONCE` behavior. Manages the AirPlay Security settings on Apple TV (Settings > AirPlay > Security). Use this payload to lock Apple TV to a particular style of AirPlay security. The setting can enable/disable an on-screen passcode, or require a specific password phrase.

declaration.title: AirPlay Security
declaration.description: The payload that configures Apple TV for a particular style of AirPlay security.

payload.content: Manages the AirPlay Security settings on Apple TV (Settings > AirPlay > Security). Use this payload to lock Apple TV to a particular style of AirPlay security. The setting can enable/disable an on-screen passcode, or require a specific password phrase.

payloadkeys.title: Security Type
payloadkeys.content:
The security policy for AirPlay. Allowed values:

- `PASSCODE_ONCE`: Requires an onscreen passcode on first connection from a device. Subsequent connections from the same device aren't prompted.
- `PASSCODE_ALWAYS`: Requires an onscreen passcode for every AirPlay connection. After an AirPlay connection ends, the system allows reconnecting within 30 seconds without a password.
- `PASSWORD`: Requires the passphrase set for `Password`.

> Note:
> `NONE` was deprecated in tvOS 11.3. Existing profiles that use `NONE` get the `PASSWORD_ONCE` behavior.

payloadkeys.title: Access Type
payloadkeys.content:
The access policy for AirPlay.

`ANY` allows connections from both Ethernet, Wi-Fi, and Apple Wireless Direct Link.

`WIFI_ONLY` allows connections only from devices on the same Ethernet or Wi-Fi network as Apple TV.

payloadkeys.title: Password
payloadkeys.content: The AirPlay password; required if `SecurityType` is `PASSWORD`.

items.title: AirPlay
items.summary: The payload that configures AirPlay settings.
items.searchKeys: Whitelist AllowListItem DeviceName DeviceID Passwords PasswordsItem DeviceID Password DeviceName AllowList
items.searchTitles: AirPlay Whitelist AllowList Content Item Device Name Device ID Passwords Password Content Item Password Device Name AllowList
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures AirPlay settings. Use `AllowList` instead. This key is deprecated in iOS 14.5 and macOS 11.3. The name of the AirPlay device.

The system limits the list of visible AirPlay destinations to devices that are present in the `AllowList` field of all installed AirPlay payloads. The device ID of the AirPlay destination in the format `xx:xx:xx:xx:xx:xx`. This field isn't case-sensitive.

The system limits the list of visible AirPlay destinations to devices that are present in the `AllowList` field of all installed AirPlay payloads.

Specifying the same MACAddress more than once, whether in the same payload across different payloads, results in undefined behavior.

As of tvOS 18, `DeviceID` isn't supported. If present, sets passwords for known AirPlay destinations. Using multiple entries for the same destination, whether within the same payload or across multiple installed payloads, is an error and results in undefined behavior. The device ID of the AirPlay destination; used in macOS.

Deprecated in macOS 15 and later as tvOS 18 AirPlay destinations don't support it; use `DeviceName` instead. The password for the AirPlay destination. The name of the AirPlay destination; used in iOS, and available in macOS 15 and later. If present, only AirPlay destinations in this list are available to the device. This allow list applies to supervised devices. macOS supports more than one payload, iOS does not. Supported on the user channel for macOS only.

items.title: AirPrint
items.summary: The payload that configures AirPrint printer discoverability in the user's printer list.
items.searchKeys: AirPrint AirPrintItem ForceTLS Port ResourcePath IPAddress
items.searchTitles: AirPrint Air print Identifier Force TLS Port Number Resource Path IP Address
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures AirPrint printer discoverability in the user's printer list. An array of AirPrint printers that are presented to the user. If `true`, AirPrint connections are secured by Transport Layer Security (TLS). Available only in iOS 11 and later. The listening port of the AirPrint destination. Available only in iOS 11 and later. The resource path associated with the printer. This path corresponds to the `rp` parameter of the `_ipps.tcp` Bonjour record. For example:

- `printers/Canon_MG5300_series`
- `printers/Xerox_Phaser_7600`
- `ipp/print`
- `Epson_IPP_Printer` The IP address or hostname of the AirPrint destination.

items.title: App Lock
items.summary: The payload that configures a device to run a single app.
items.searchKeys: App UserEnabledOptions AssistiveTouch InvertColors Zoom VoiceOver VoiceControl Options EnableVoiceControl EnableMonoAudio EnableSpeakSelection EnableAssistiveTouch EnableInvertColors EnableZoom EnableVoiceOver DisableAutoLock DisableSleepWakeButton DisableRingerSwitch DisableVolumeButtons DisableDeviceRotation DisableTouch Identifier
items.searchTitles: App Lock  App User Enabled Options Assistive Touch Invert Colors Zoom Voice Over Voice Control Options Enable Voice Control Enable Mono Audio Enable Speak Selection Enable Assistive Touch Enable Invert Colors Enable Zoom Enable Voice Over Disable Auto Lock Disable Sleep Wake Button Disable Ringer Switch Disable Volume Buttons Disable Device Rotation Disable Touch Identifier
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures a device to run a single app. With an app lock profile, the device locks to the specified app until removal of the profile. The device returns to the app automatically upon wake or restart.

Only use an app lock payload after installing the target app. A dictionary that contains information about the app. A dictionary of user-editable options. If `true`, the system allows the user to toggle AssistiveTouch. If `true`, the system allows the user to toggle Invert Colors. If `true`, the system allows the user to toggle Zoom. If `true`, the system allows the user to toggle VoiceOver. If `true`, the system allows the user to toggle Voice Control. A dictionary of options that the user can't change. If `true`, the system enables Voice Control. If `true`, the system enables Mono Audio. If `true`, the system enables Speak Selection. If `true`, the system enables AssistiveTouch. If `true`, the system enables Invert Colors. If `true`, the system enables Zoom. If `true`, the system enables VoiceOver. If `true`, the device doesn't automatically go to sleep after an idle period. If `true`, the system disables the sleep/wake button. If `true`, the system disables the ringer switch. When disabled, the ringer behavior depends on what position the switch was in when it was first disabled. If `true`, the system disables the volume buttons. If `true`, the system disables device rotation sensing. If `true`, the system disables the touch screen. In tvOS, it disables the touch surface on the Apple TV Remote. The app's bundle identifier.

items.title: Parental Controls: Application Restrictions
items.summary: The payload that configures parental controls for apps.
items.searchKeys: pathWhiteList pathWhiteListItem pathBlackList pathBlackListItem whiteList whiteListItem displayName subApps disabled detachedSignature appID bundleID familyControlsEnabled
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures parental controls for apps. To determine if an app can be launched, the app is evaluated with these rules:

- Certain system app and utilities are always allowed to run.
- The allow list is searched to see if the `bundleID` has a matching entry. If a match is found, `appID` and `detachedSignature` (if present) are used to verify the signature of the app being launched. If the signature is valid and matches the designated requirement (in the appID key), the app is allowed to launch.
- If the path to the binary being launched matches or is in a subdirectory of a path in the deny list, the binary is denied.
- If the path to the binary being launched matches or is a subdirectory of a path in the allow list, the binary is allowed to launch.
- The binary is denied permission to launch. The paths to apps in the allow list. This property is deprecated in macOS 10.15 and later. A path. The paths to apps in the deny list. This property is deprecated in macOS 10.15 and later. A path. The allow list of app item dictionaries. A dictionary defining an app for parental control. The name used for display purposes. An array of nested helper applications. If `true`, this app isn't added to the allow list. The signature for an unsigned binary. The identifier of the app. Obtain this value from the Security framework using `SecCodeCopyDesignatedRequirement`. The bundle ID of the app. If `true`, enables app access restrictions. Parental controls application restrictions.
Order of evaluation:
(1) Certain system applications and utilities are always allowed to run
(2) The "whiteList" is searched to see if a matching entry is found by bundleID. If a match is found, the "appID" and "detachedSignature"
(if present) are used to verify the signature of the application being launched. If the signature is valid and matches the designated
requirement (in the "appID" key), the application is allowed to launch.
(3) (deprecated) If the path to the binary being launched matches (or is in a subdirectory) of a path in "pathBlackList", the binary is denied.
(4) (deprecated) If the path to the binary being launched matches (or is a subdirectory) of a path in "pathWhiteList", the binary is allowed to launch.
(5) The binary is denied permission to launch.

declaration.title: Parental Controls: Application Restrictions

(if present) are used to verify the signature of the application being launched. If the signature is valid and matches the designated
requirement (in the "appID" key), the application is allowed to launch.
(3) (deprecated) If the path to the binary being launched matches (or is in a subdirectory) of a path in "pathBlackList", the binary is denied.
(4) (deprecated) If the path to the binary being launched matches (or is a subdirectory) of a path in "pathWhiteList", the binary is allowed to launch.
(5) The binary is denied permission to launch.
