items.title: Google Account
items.summary: The payload that configures a Google account.
items.searchKeys: VPNUUID CommunicationServiceRules DefaultServiceHandlers AudioCall EmailAddress AccountName AccountDescription
items.searchTitles: Google Account  VPNUUID Communication Service Rules Default Service Handlers App for audio calls Email Address Account Name Account Description
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures a Google account. You can install multiple Google payloads. Each sets up a Google email address and any other Google services the user enables after authentication.

> Note:
> For supervised devices, the system requires installation of Google accounts through MDM or Apple Configurator 2.

The payload never contains credentials; the system prompts the user to enter credentials shortly after installation of the payload. The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later. The communication service handler rules for this account. A dictionary that defines which app to use for audio calls from this account. The bundle identifier for the default application that handles audio calls to contacts from this account. The full Google email address for the account. The user's full name for the Google account. This name appears in sent messages. A user-visible description of the Google account, shown in the Mail and Settings apps. A Google account payload sets up a Google email address as well as any other Google services the user enables after authentication. Google accounts must be installed via MDM or by Apple Configurator 2 (if the device is supervised). The payload never contains credentials and the user will be prompted to enter their credentials shortly after the payload successfully installs. On Shared iPads, this payload can only be installed on the MDM user channel.

items.title: Home Screen Layout
items.summary: The payload that configures the Home Screen layout.
items.searchKeys: Pages PagesItem IconItem URL Pages BundleID DisplayName Type Dock
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures the Home Screen layout. This payload defines a layout of apps, folders, and web clips for the Home Screen. This layout is locked and can't be modified by the user.

If a Home Screen layout puts more than four items in the iPhone Dock the location of the fifth and succeeding items may be undefined but they will not be omitted.

To disable deletion of apps, set `allowAppRemoval` to `false` with `Restrictions`. An array of arrays of dictionaries, each of which must conform to the icon dictionary format. An array of dictionaries that conform to the icon dictionary format. The URL of the existing web clip for this item. This setting is required if `type` is `WebClip`. If more than one web clip exists with the same URL, the behavior is undefined.

Specifying a web clip in this payload doesn't create the web clip. Use the `WebClip` payload to create a web clip. An array of arrays of dictionaries, each conforming to the icon dictionary format. This setting is valid only if the type is `Folder`. The bundle identifier of the app. This setting is required if the type is `Application`. The human-readable string shown to the user. This setting is valid only if the type is `Folder`. The type of the Dock item. An array of dictionaries, each of which must conform to the icon dictionary format. If this key isn't present, the user's Dock is empty. The payload defines a layout of apps, folders, & web clips for the Home screen.

The URL of the existing web clip for this item. This setting is required if `type` is `WebClip`. If more than one web clip exists with the same URL, the behavior is undefined.

Specifying a web clip in this payload doesn't create the web clip. Use the `WebClip` payload to create a web clip.

notes.content:
This payload defines a layout of apps, folders, and web clips for the Home Screen. This layout is locked and can't be modified by the user.

If a Home Screen layout puts more than four items in the iPhone Dock the location of the fifth and succeeding items may be undefined but they will not be omitted.

To disable deletion of apps, set `allowAppRemoval` to `false` with `Restrictions`.

- `LDAPSearchSettingScopeBase`: The search uses only the immediate node that the search base points to.

- `LDAPSearchSettingScopeSubtree`: The search uses the node plus all children, regardless of depth. The path to the node where a search should start. The description of this search setting. items.title: Lights Out Management (LOM)
items.summary: The payload that configures lights-out management (LOM) settings.
items.searchKeys: ControllerCACertificateUUIDs ControllerCACertificateUUIDsItem DeviceCACertificateUUIDs DeviceCACertificateUUIDsItem ControllerCertificateUUID DeviceCertificateUUID
items.searchTitles: Lights Out Management (LOM)  CA certificate payload UUIDs CA certificate payload UUIDs Controller Certificate payload UUID Device Certificate payload UUID
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures lights-out management (LOM) settings. You can configure a compatible macOS device to be both a controller and a device. Include the UUID of the certificate in both `DeviceCertificateUUID` and `ControllerCertificateUUID` properties. An array of payload UUIDs containing CA certificates that devices use to evaluate trust of controller certificates.

This key configures the device to accept the `LOMDeviceRequestCommand` from MDM and then send it to the target device. This certificate must contain the Key Usage attributes of Digital Signature, Key Encipherment and Data Encipherment. As well as the Extended Key Usage attributes of Server Authentication and Client Authentication. An array of payload UUIDs containing CA certificates that controllers use to evaluate trust of device certificates. The UUID certificate for the LOM controller. This key configures the device to accept the `LOMDeviceRequestCommand` from MDM and then send it to the target device. The UUID certificate for the device. This key indicates the device can receive `PowerON`, `PowerOFF`, and `Reset` requests from a LOM controller. This certificate must contain the Key Usage attributes of Digital Signature, Key Encipherment and Data Encipherment. As well as the Extended Key Usage attributes of Server Authentication and Client Authentication. Configures a computer to send or receive "PowerON". "PowerOFF", "Reset" requests.

As well as the Extended Key Usage attributes of Server Authentication and Client Authentication.

items.summary: The payload that configures a Mail account.
items.searchKeys: VPNUUID SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch SMIMEEncryptionCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable SMIMEEncryptByDefaultUserOverrideable SMIMEEncryptByDefault SMIMESigningCertificateUUIDUserOverrideable SMIMESigningUserOverrideable IncomingMailServerIMAPPathPrefix allowMailDrop disableMailRecentsSyncing SMIMEEnablePerMessageSwitch SMIMEEncryptionCertificateUUID SMIMEEncryptionEnabled SMIMESigningCertificateUUID SMIMESigningEnabled SMIMEEnabled PreventAppSheet PreventMove OutgoingMailServerUsername OutgoingMailServerUseSSL OutgoingMailServerPortNumber OutgoingMailServerHostName OutgoingMailServerAuthentication OutgoingPasswordSameAsIncomingPassword OutgoingPassword IncomingPassword IncomingMailServerUsername IncomingMailServerUseSSL IncomingMailServerPortNumber IncomingMailServerHostName IncomingMailServerAuthentication EmailAddress EmailAccountType EmailAccountName EmailAccountDescription
items.searchTitles: Mail VPNUUID Path Prefix Allow Mail Drop Disable Mail Recents Syncing S/MIME Enable Per-Message Switch S/MIME Encryption Certificate S/MIME Encryption Enabled S/MIME Signing Certificate S/MIME Signing Enabled S/MIME Enabled Prevent App Sheet Prevent Move Username Use SSL Port Mail Server Authentication Type Outgoing Password Same As Incoming Password Password Username Use SSL Port Mail Server Incoming Mail Server Authentication Email Address Account Type Account Name Account Description
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures a Mail account. The VPNUUID of the per-app VPN the account uses for network communication. Available in iOS 14 and later. If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI. If `true`, the user can select the S/MIME encryption identity, and encryption is on. If `true`, the user can turn encryption by default on/off, and encryption is on. If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption by default. If `true`, the user can select the signing identity. If `true`, the user can turn S/MIME signing on or off in Settings. The path prefix for the IMAP mail server. If `true`, the system enables this account to use Mail Drop. If `true`, the system excludes this account from Recent Addresses syncing. If `true`, the system displays the per-message encryption switch in the Mail Compose UI. Deprecated in iOS 12.0. Use `SMIMEEnableEncryptionPerMessageSwitch` instead. The UUID of the identity certificate used to decrypt messages sent to this account. The system attaches the public certificate to outgoing mail to allow the user to receive encrypted mail. When the user sends encrypted mail, the system uses the public certificate to encrypt the copy of the mail in their Sent mailbox. If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption for this account. The payload UUID of the identity certificate used to sign messages sent from this account. If `true`, the system enables S/MIME signing for this account. If `true`, the system enables S/MIME encryption. The system ignores this key in iOS 10.0 and later. If `true`, the system prevents this account from sending mail in any app other than the Apple Mail app. If `true`, the system prevents moving messages out of this email account and into another account. It also prevents forwarding or replying from an account other than the recipient of the message. The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for outgoing email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences. If `true`, the system enables SSL authentication on the outgoing mail server. The outgoing mail server port number. If not set, the system uses ports 25, 587, and 465, in that order. The outgoing mail server host name. The authentication scheme for outgoing mail. If `true`, the system prompts the user only once for the password, which it uses for both outgoing and incoming mail.

This setting is only supported by interactive profile installations. Not supported by non-interactive installations, such as MDM on iOS. The password for the outgoing mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles. The password for the incoming mail server. Only use this in encrypted profiles. The user name for the email account, usually the same as the email address up to the "@" character. If not set and the account requires authentication for incoming email, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences. If `true`, the system enables SSL for authentication on the incoming mail server. The incoming mail server port number. If not set, the system uses the default port for a given protocol. The incoming mail server host name. The authentication scheme for incoming mail. The full email address for the account. If this string isn't present in the payload, the device prompts the user for this string during interactive profile installation in Settings or System Preferences. Defines the protocol to use for the account. The full user name for the account. The system displays this name in sent messages. A user-visible description of the email account, shown in the Mail and Settings applications. An email payload creates an email account on the device.

items.title: Login Window: Scripts
items.summary: The payload that configures scripts to run at login and logout.
items.searchKeys: skipLogoutHook skipLoginHook logoutscripts ScriptsItems filedata filename loginscripts
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures scripts to run at login and logout. The MCX login and logout managed-scripts payload contains information about executable scripts that can run at user login and logout. To use this payload, set `EnableMCXLoginScripts` to `true` in `/var/root/Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow.plist`; otherwise, the system ignores this payload.

`Loginwindow` uses the `LoginHook` and `LogoutHook` string keys in `/var/root/Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow.plist` to indicate a path to the executable script files, which run during user login and logout. The system passes the current user name as an argument to the file. If `true`, the system doesn't execute the logout scripts during logout. If `true`, the system doesn't execute the login scripts during login. An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user logout time. A dictionary of login scripts. The UTF-8 encoded data object representing the executable script. The filename for display purposes. An array of one or more dictionaries of scripts to run at user login time. Login and logout managed script handling

If `true`, the system doesn't execute the logout scripts during logout.

The MCX login and logout managed-scripts payload contains information about executable scripts that can run at user login and logout. To use this payload, set `EnableMCXLoginScripts` to `true` in `/var/root/Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow.plist`; otherwise, the system ignores this payload.

`Loginwindow` uses the `LoginHook` and `LogoutHook` string keys in `/var/root/Library/Preferences/com.apple.loginwindow.plist` to indicate a path to the executable script files, which run during user login and logout. The system passes the current user name as an argument to the file.

items.summary: The payload that configures mobile device management (MDM) settings.

items.searchContent:
The payload that configures mobile device management (MDM) settings. Also define the following four standard payload values in your MDM payload:

- `PayloadIdentifier`: The reverse-DNS style identifier that identifies the profile; for example, `com.example.myprofile`. The system uses this value to determine whether to replace an existing profile or add a new one.
- `PayloadUUID`: A globally unique identifier for the profile. In macOS, you can use `uuidgen` to generate this value.
- `PayloadType`: The payload type. Set to `com.apple.mdm` to designate that this payload is an MDM payload.
- `PayloadVersion`: The version number of the profile format, which describes the version of the configuration profile as a whole, not of the individual profiles within it. Set this value to `1`.



> Note:
> MDM reserves profile payload dictionary keys with the _Payload_ prefix. Don't treat them as managed preferences. If 'true', the system warns the user that they need to reboot into RecoveryOS and allow the MDM to use the bootstrap token for authentication for certain sensitive operations such as enabling kernel extensions or installing some types of software updates. If the MDM doesn't need to perform these operations, it can leave this key set to 'false', and the user isn't notified.
The SettingsCommand.Command.Settings.MDMOptions.MDMOptions command overrides this default value.
This setting only applies to devices that have 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForSoftwareUpdate' or 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForKernelExtensionApproval' set to 'true' in their SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo.
DEP-enrolled devices are automatically allowed to use the bootstrap token for authentication.
Available in macOS 11 and later. This property specifies an iTunes Store ID for an app the system can install with the InstallApplicationCommand, without any approval from the user. The MDM vendor or managing organization generally provides this app, which enhances the management experience for the user. The device shows the user details about this app in the account-driven enrollment process prior to installing the MDM profile. Use this property with account-driven MDM enrollments that normally require user approval for app installs through MDM.
Only account-driven enrollments support this property and other enrollment types ignore it.
Available in iOS 15.1 and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. If 'true', the device attempts to send a `Check-Out` message to the 'CheckInURL' when the profile is removed. A unique array of strings indicating server capabilities:

- `com.apple.mdm.per-user-connections`: Indicates that the server supports both device and user connections. This must be present when managing Shared iPad or macOS devices.
- `com.apple.mdm.bootstraptoken`: Indicates that the server supports escrowing the bootstrap token. This must be present for the device to create a bootstrap token and send it to the server. Available in iOS 26 and later, macOS 11 and later, and visionOS 26 and later.
- `com.apple.mdm.token`: Indicates that the server supports the `Get-Token` CheckIn message type. This must be present for the device to use `Get-Token` CheckIn message when appropriate.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the `com.apple.mdm.per-user-connections` capability must not be added or removed. Any such change is an error, and the update is rejected. If 'true', the system fails the connection attempt unless it obtains a verified positive response during certificate revocation checks.
If 'false', the system performs revocation checks on a best-attempt basis, where failure to reach the server isn't considered fatal. An array of strings, each containing the payload UUID of a certificate to use when evaluating trust to the '.../checkin/' URLs of MDM servers. A certificate payload UUID. An array of strings, each containing the UUID of a certificate to use when evaluating trust to the '.../connect/' URLs of MDM servers. A certificate payload UUID. The enrollment mode the server indicates to use when enrolling. Required for account-driven enrollment. Available in iOS 15 and macOS 14, and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. The Managed Apple Account pre-assigned to the authenticated user. Required for account-driven enrollments. Available in iOS 15 and later, and macOS 14 and later.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. The Managed Apple Account of the user. Previously required for profile-driven user enrollment.
Removed as of iOS 18 and macOS 15. If 'true', the device uses the development APNS servers. Otherwise, the device uses the production servers.
Set to 'false' if your Apple Push Notification Service certificate was issued by the Apple Push Certificate Portal ('https://identity.apple.com/pushcert'). That portal only issues certificates for the production push environment. Logical OR of the following bit flags:

- `1`: Allow inspection of installed configuration profiles.
- `2`: Allow installation and removal of configuration profiles.
- `4`: Allow device lock and passcode removal.
- `8`: Allow device erase.
- `16`: Allow query of device information (device capacity, serial number).
- `32`: Allow query of network information (phone/SIM numbers, MAC addresses).
- `64`: Allow inspection of installed provisioning profiles.
- `128`: Allow installation and removal of provisioning profiles.
- `256`: Allow inspection of installed applications.
- `512`: Allow restriction-related queries.
- `1024`: Allow security-related queries.
- `2048`: Allow manipulation of settings.
- `4096`: Allow app management.

Don't set to `0`. Specify `1` if you specify `2`. Specify `64` if you specify `128`. Ignored if you set a value for `ManagedAppleID`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the addition of any access right is an error, and the update is rejected. If 'true', each message coming from the device carries the additional 'Mdm-Signature' HTTP header. The URL that the device should use to check in during installation. The URL must begin with the `https://` URL scheme and may contain a port number (`:1234`, for example). If not set, the system uses `ServerURL`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. The URL that the device contacts to retrieve device management instructions. The URL must begin with the `https://` URL scheme, and may contain a port number
(`:1234`, for example).

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. The topic that MDM listens to for push notifications. The certificate that the server uses to send push notifications must have the same topic in its subject. The topic must begin with the 'com.apple.mgmt.' prefix.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected. The UUID of the certificate payload for the device's identity. It may also point to a SCEP payload.

(`:1234`, for example).

payloadkeys.title: Check In URL
payloadkeys.content:
The URL that the device should use to check in during installation. The URL must begin with the `https://` URL scheme and may contain a port number (`:1234`, for example). If not set, the system uses `ServerURL`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the value of this key must not change. Any change is an error, and the update is rejected.

payloadkeys.title: Sign Message
payloadkeys.content: If 'true', each message coming from the device carries the additional 'Mdm-Signature' HTTP header.

payloadkeys.title: Access Rights
payloadkeys.content:
Logical OR of the following bit flags:

- `1`: Allow inspection of installed configuration profiles.
- `2`: Allow installation and removal of configuration profiles.
- `4`: Allow device lock and passcode removal.
- `8`: Allow device erase.
- `16`: Allow query of device information (device capacity, serial number).
- `32`: Allow query of network information (phone/SIM numbers, MAC addresses).
- `64`: Allow inspection of installed provisioning profiles.
- `128`: Allow installation and removal of provisioning profiles.
- `256`: Allow inspection of installed applications.
- `512`: Allow restriction-related queries.
- `1024`: Allow security-related queries.
- `2048`: Allow manipulation of settings.
- `4096`: Allow app management.

Don't set to `0`. Specify `1` if you specify `2`. Specify `64` if you specify `128`. Ignored if you set a value for `ManagedAppleID`.

> Note:
> When updating the payload, the addition of any access right is an error, and the update is rejected.

payloadkeys.title: Use Development APNS

If 'true', the device uses the development APNS servers. Otherwise, the device uses the production servers.
Set to 'false' if your Apple Push Notification Service certificate was issued by the Apple Push Certificate Portal ('https://identity.apple.com/pushcert'). That portal only issues certificates for the production push environment.

If 'false', the system performs revocation checks on a best-attempt basis, where failure to reach the server isn't considered fatal.

A unique array of strings indicating server capabilities:

- `com.apple.mdm.per-user-connections`: Indicates that the server supports both device and user connections. This must be present when managing Shared iPad or macOS devices.
- `com.apple.mdm.bootstraptoken`: Indicates that the server supports escrowing the bootstrap token. This must be present for the device to create a bootstrap token and send it to the server. Available in iOS 26 and later, macOS 11 and later, and visionOS 26 and later.
- `com.apple.mdm.token`: Indicates that the server supports the `Get-Token` CheckIn message type. This must be present for the device to use `Get-Token` CheckIn message when appropriate.

> Note:

If 'true', the device attempts to send a `Check-Out` message to the 'CheckInURL' when the profile is removed.

The MDM vendor or managing organization generally provides this app, which enhances the management experience for the user. The device shows the user details about this app in the account-driven enrollment process prior to installing the MDM profile. Use this property with account-driven MDM enrollments that normally require user approval for app installs through MDM.
Only account-driven enrollments support this property and other enrollment types ignore it.
Available in iOS 15.1 and later.

> Note:

If 'true', the system warns the user that they need to reboot into RecoveryOS and allow the MDM to use the bootstrap token for authentication for certain sensitive operations such as enabling kernel extensions or installing some types of software updates. If the MDM doesn't need to perform these operations, it can leave this key set to 'false', and the user isn't notified.
The SettingsCommand.Command.Settings.MDMOptions.MDMOptions command overrides this default value.
This setting only applies to devices that have 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForSoftwareUpdate' or 'BootstrapTokenRequiredForKernelExtensionApproval' set to 'true' in their SecurityInfoResponse.SecurityInfo.

notes.content:
Also define the following four standard payload values in your MDM payload:

- `PayloadIdentifier`: The reverse-DNS style identifier that identifies the profile; for example, `com.example.myprofile`. The system uses this value to determine whether to replace an existing profile or add a new one.
- `PayloadUUID`: A globally unique identifier for the profile. In macOS, you can use `uuidgen` to generate this value.
- `PayloadType`: The payload type. Set to `com.apple.mdm` to designate that this payload is an MDM payload.
- `PayloadVersion`: The version number of the profile format, which describes the version of the configuration profile as a whole, not of the individual profiles within it. Set this value to `1`.



> Note:

items.title: Passcode
items.summary: The payload that configures a passcode policy.
items.searchKeys: customRegex passwordContentDescription ANY passwordContentRegex changeAtNextAuth minutesUntilFailedLoginReset maxGracePeriod pinHistory requireAlphanumeric minLength minComplexChars maxPINAgeInDays maxInactivity maxFailedAttempts forcePIN allowSimple
items.searchTitles: Passcode  Grace Period for Device Lock Passcode History Require Alphabetic Value Minimum Passcode Length Minimum Number of Complex Characters Maximum Passcode Age Auto-Lock Maximum Number of Failed Attempts Require Passcode on Device Allow Simple Value
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures a passcode policy. The presence of this payload type causes the device to present the user with a passcode entry mechanism. The payload controls the complexity of the passcode.

For user enrollments, the system allows this payload type, but ignores most of the keys. Instead, the presence of the payload forces only these settings:

- `allowSimple`: always set to `false`
- `forcePIN`: always set to `true`
- `minLength`: always set to `6`
- `maxInactivity`: if this key is present its value is ignored, but the `never` option is removed in the Settings UI. Specifies a regular expression, and its description, used to enforce password compliance. Use the simpler passcode restrictions whenever possible, and rely on regular expression matching only when necessary. Mistakes in regular expressions can lead to frustrating user experiences, such as unsatisfiable passcode policies, or policy descriptions that don't match the enforced policy.

Available in macOS 14 and later. Contains a dictionary of keys for supported OS language IDs (for example, "en-US"), and whose values represent a localized description of the policy enforced by the regular expression. Use the special `default` key can for languages that aren't contained in the dictionary. A localized description. A regular expression string that the system matches against the password to determine whether it complies with a policy. The regular expression uses the ICU syntax ([https://unicode-org.github.io/icu/userguide/strings/regexp.html](https://unicode-org.github.io/icu/userguide/strings/regexp.html)). The string must not exceed 2048 characters in length. If `true`, the system causes a password reset to occur the next time the user tries to authenticate. If this key is set in a device profile, the setting takes effect for all users, and admin authentications may fail until the admin user password is also reset. Available in macOS 10.13 and later. The number of minutes before the system resets the login after the maximum number of unsuccessful login attempts is reached. This key requires setting `maxFailedAttempts`. Available in macOS 10.10 and later. The maximum grace period, in minutes, to unlock the phone without entering a passcode. The default is `0`, which is no grace period and requires a passcode immediately. On macOS, the system translates this grace period value to screen-saver settings. This value defines _N_, where the new passcode must be unique within the last _N_ entries in the passcode history. If `true`, the system requires alphabetic characters instead of only numeric characters. The minimum overall length of the passcode. This value is independent of the value for `minComplexChars`. The minimum number of complex characters that a passcode needs to contain. A _complex_ character is a character other than a number or a letter, such as `&`, `%`, `$`, and `#`.

The system ignores this property for user enrollments. The number of days for which the passcode can remain unchanged. After this number of days, the system forces the user to change the passcode before it unlocks the device. The maximum number of minutes for which the device can be idle without the user unlocking it, before the system locks it. When this limit is reached, the system locks the device and the passcode is required to unlock it. The user can edit this setting, but the value can't exceed the `maxInactivity` value.

On macOS, the system translates this inactivity value to screen-saver settings. The maximum value for macOS is `60`.

Setting this key removes the `never` option in the Settings UI on user enrolled devices. The number of failed passcode attempts that the system allows the user before it erases or locks the device. After six failed attempts, the device imposes a time delay before the user can enter a passcode again. The time delay increases with each failed attempt. On macOS, set `minutesUntilFailedLoginReset` to define the time delay. The time delay begins after the sixth attempt, so if `MaximumFailedAttempts` is six or lower, the system has no time delay and triggers the erase or lock as soon as the user exceeds the limit.

After the final failed attempt, the system locks a macOS device, or securely erases all data and settings from an iOS, visionOS, or watchOS device. If `true`, the system forces the user to enter a PIN. If `false`, the system prevents use of a simple passcode. A simple passcode contains repeated characters, or increasing or decreasing characters, such as `123` or `CBA`.

declaration.title: Passcode
declaration.description: The payload that configures a passcode policy.

payloadkeys.title: Allow Simple Value
payloadkeys.content: If `false`, the system prevents use of a simple passcode. A simple passcode contains repeated characters, or increasing or decreasing characters, such as `123` or `CBA`.

payloadkeys.title: Require Passcode on Device
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system forces the user to enter a PIN.

payloadkeys.title: Maximum Number of Failed Attempts
payloadkeys.content:
The number of failed passcode attempts that the system allows the user before it erases or locks the device. After six failed attempts, the device imposes a time delay before the user can enter a passcode again. The time delay increases with each failed attempt. On macOS, set `minutesUntilFailedLoginReset` to define the time delay. The time delay begins after the sixth attempt, so if `MaximumFailedAttempts` is six or lower, the system has no time delay and triggers the erase or lock as soon as the user exceeds the limit.

After the final failed attempt, the system locks a macOS device, or securely erases all data and settings from an iOS, visionOS, or watchOS device.

payloadkeys.title: Auto-Lock
payloadkeys.content:
The maximum number of minutes for which the device can be idle without the user unlocking it, before the system locks it. When this limit is reached, the system locks the device and the passcode is required to unlock it. The user can edit this setting, but the value can't exceed the `maxInactivity` value.

On macOS, the system translates this inactivity value to screen-saver settings. The maximum value for macOS is `60`.

Setting this key removes the `never` option in the Settings UI on user enrolled devices.

payloadkeys.title: Maximum Passcode Age
payloadkeys.content: The number of days for which the passcode can remain unchanged. After this number of days, the system forces the user to change the passcode before it unlocks the device.

payloadkeys.title: Minimum Number of Complex Characters
payloadkeys.content:
The minimum number of complex characters that a passcode needs to contain. A _complex_ character is a character other than a number or a letter, such as `&`, `%`, `$`, and `#`.

The system ignores this property for user enrollments.

payloadkeys.title: Minimum Passcode Length
payloadkeys.content: The minimum overall length of the passcode. This value is independent of the value for `minComplexChars`.

payloadkeys.title: Require Alphabetic Value
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the system requires alphabetic characters instead of only numeric characters.

payloadkeys.title: Passcode History
payloadkeys.content: This value defines _N_, where the new passcode must be unique within the last _N_ entries in the passcode history.

payloadkeys.title: Grace Period for Device Lock
payloadkeys.content: The maximum grace period, in minutes, to unlock the phone without entering a passcode. The default is `0`, which is no grace period and requires a passcode immediately. On macOS, the system translates this grace period value to screen-saver settings.

The number of minutes before the system resets the login after the maximum number of unsuccessful login attempts is reached. This key requires setting `maxFailedAttempts`. Available in macOS 10.10 and later.

If `true`, the system causes a password reset to occur the next time the user tries to authenticate. If this key is set in a device profile, the setting takes effect for all users, and admin authentications may fail until the admin user password is also reset. Available in macOS 10.13 and later.

Specifies a regular expression, and its description, used to enforce password compliance. Use the simpler passcode restrictions whenever possible, and rely on regular expression matching only when necessary. Mistakes in regular expressions can lead to frustrating user experiences, such as unsatisfiable passcode policies, or policy descriptions that don't match the enforced policy.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.content: A regular expression string that the system matches against the password to determine whether it complies with a policy. The regular expression uses the ICU syntax ([https://unicode-org.github.io/icu/userguide/strings/regexp.html](https://unicode-org.github.io/icu/userguide/strings/regexp.html)). The string must not exceed 2048 characters in length.

Contains a dictionary of keys for supported OS language IDs (for example, "en-US"), and whose values represent a localized description of the policy enforced by the regular expression. Use the special `default` key can for languages that aren't contained in the dictionary.
A localized description.

notes.content:
The presence of this payload type causes the device to present the user with a passcode entry mechanism. The payload controls the complexity of the passcode.

For user enrollments, the system allows this payload type, but ignores most of the keys. Instead, the presence of the payload forces only these settings:

- `allowSimple`: always set to `false`
- `forcePIN`: always set to `true`
- `minLength`: always set to `6`
- `maxInactivity`: if this key is present its value is ignored, but the `never` option is removed in the Settings UI.

items.title: Network Usage Rules
items.summary: The payload that configures network-usage rules.
items.searchKeys: SIMRules SIMRulesItem WiFiAssistPolicy ICCIDs ICCID ApplicationRules ApplicationRulesItem AllowCellularData AllowRoamingCellularData AppIdentifierMatches AppIdentifierMatchesItem
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures network-usage rules. Network usage rules allow enterprises to specify how devices use networks, such as cellular data networks. iOS 9-12 require the application rules. In iOS 13, application rules, SIM rules, or both must be present. An array of SIM rules, that apply to all apps. The policy for individual SIM cards. The Wi-Fi Assist policy to apply to the SIM cards specified in the ICCIDs. Allowed values:

- `2`: Use the default system policy for the specified SIM card(s).
- `3`: Make Wi-Fi Assist switch more aggressively from a poor Wi-Fi connection to cellular data for the specified SIM card(s). This setting may increase cellular data use and may impact battery life.

For more information, see [About Wi-Fi Assist](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT205296). One or more ICCIDs of SIM cards for which the `WiFiAssistPolicy` applies. All ICCIDs in all installed Network Usage Rules payloads must be unique. An example ICCID is `89310410106543789301`. An ICCID. An array of application rules, that apply to only managed apps. The application rules dictionary. If `false`, disables cellular data for all matching managed apps. If `false`, disables cellular data while roaming for all matching managed apps. A list of managed app identifiers, as strings, that must follow the associated rules. If this key is missing, the rules apply to all managed apps on the device.



Each string in the `AppIdentifierMatches` array may either be an exact app identifier match (for example, `com.mycompany.myapp`) or it may specify a prefix match for the bundle ID by using the \* wildcard character. If used, this character must appear after a period (.) and may only appear once, at the end of the string; for example, `com.mycompany.*`. A managed app identifier. Network Usage Rules allow enterprises to specify how devices use networks, such as cellular data networks. iOS 9-12 support only ApplicationRules. In iOS 13, ApplicationRules, SIMRules, or both must be present.

declaration.title: Network Usage Rules
declaration.description: The payload that configures network-usage rules.

payload.content: Network Usage Rules allow enterprises to specify how devices use networks, such as cellular data networks. iOS 9-12 support only ApplicationRules. In iOS 13, ApplicationRules, SIMRules, or both must be present.

payloadkeys.content: An array of application rules, that apply to only managed apps.

subkeys.content: The application rules dictionary.

A list of managed app identifiers, as strings, that must follow the associated rules. If this key is missing, the rules apply to all managed apps on the device.



Each string in the `AppIdentifierMatches` array may either be an exact app identifier match (for example, `com.mycompany.myapp`) or it may specify a prefix match for the bundle ID by using the \* wildcard character. If used, this character must appear after a period (.) and may only appear once, at the end of the string; for example, `com.mycompany.*`.
A managed app identifier.
If `false`, disables cellular data while roaming for all matching managed apps.
If `false`, disables cellular data for all matching managed apps.

payloadkeys.content: An array of SIM rules, that apply to all apps.

subkeys.content: The policy for individual SIM cards.
One or more ICCIDs of SIM cards for which the `WiFiAssistPolicy` applies. All ICCIDs in all installed Network Usage Rules payloads must be unique. An example ICCID is `89310410106543789301`.
An ICCID.

The Wi-Fi Assist policy to apply to the SIM cards specified in the ICCIDs. Allowed values:

- `2`: Use the default system policy for the specified SIM card(s).
- `3`: Make Wi-Fi Assist switch more aggressively from a poor Wi-Fi connection to cellular data for the specified SIM card(s). This setting may increase cellular data use and may impact battery life.

For more information, see [About Wi-Fi Assist](https://support.apple.com/en-us/HT205296).

notes.content: Network usage rules allow enterprises to specify how devices use networks, such as cellular data networks. iOS 9-12 require the application rules. In iOS 13, application rules, SIM rules, or both must be present.

items.searchTitles: Notifications Notification Settings Notification Setting Preview Type Grouping Type Critical Alert Enabled Show in CarPlay Sounds Enabled Badges Enabled Alert Type Show in Lock Screen Show in Notification Center Enable Notifications App Bundle Identifier
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures notifications. An array of notification settings dictionaries. The type previews for notifications. This key overrides the value at Settings>Notifications>Show Previews.

- `0` - Always: Previews will be shown when the device is locked and unlocked
- `1` - When Unlocked: Previews will only be shown when the device is unlocked
- `2` - Never: Previews will never be shown

Available in iOS 14 and later. The type of grouping for notifications for this app:

- `0`: Automatic: Group notifications into app-specified groups.
- `1`: By app: Group notifications into one group.
- `2`: Off: Don't group notifications.

Available in iOS 12 and later. If `true`, enables critical alerts that can ignore Do Not Disturb and ringer settings for this app.

Available in iOS 12 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, enables notifications in CarPlay for this app.

Available in iOS 12 and later. If `true`, enables sounds for this app. If `true`, enables badges for this app.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. The type of alert for notifications for this app:

- `0`: None
- `1`: Temporary Banner
- `2`: Persistent Banner

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, enables notifications on the Lock Screen for this app.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, enables notifications in the notification center for this app.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. If `true`, enables notifications for this app.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. The bundle identifier of the app to which to apply these notification settings.

Available in iOS 9.3 and later and macOS 10.15 and later. A notification settings payload specifies the restriction enforced notification settings for apps using their bundle identifier. The profile specifies notification settings by bundle identifier (even for apps that aren’t installed on the device yet), and those settings will always be enforced.

The profile specifies notification settings by bundle identifier (even for apps that aren’t installed on the device yet), and those settings will always be enforced.

items.title: Profile Removal Password
items.summary: The payload that configures profile removal.
items.searchKeys: RemovalPassword
items.searchContent: The payload that configures profile removal. This payload provides a password to allow users to remove a locked configuration profile from the device. If this payload is present and has a password value set, the device asks for the password when the user taps a profile's Remove button. This system encrypts the payload with the rest of the profile. The password to allow removing the profile.

declaration.description: The payload that configures profile removal.

payloadkeys.content: The password to allow removing the profile.

notes.content: This payload provides a password to allow users to remove a locked configuration profile from the device. If this payload is present and has a password value set, the device asks for the password when the user taps a profile's Remove button. This system encrypts the payload with the rest of the profile.

items.title: Global HTTP Proxy
items.summary: The payload that configures a global HTTP proxy.
items.searchKeys: ProxyCaptiveLoginAllowed ProxyPACFallbackAllowed ProxyPACURL ProxyPassword ProxyUsername ProxyServerPort ProxyServer ProxyType
items.searchTitles: Global HTTP Proxy  Proxy Bypass Allowed Proxy PAC Fallback Allowed Proxy PAC URL Proxy Password Proxy Username Proxy Server Port Proxy Server Proxy Type
items.searchContent: The payload that configures a global HTTP proxy. There can only be one payload of this type on the device at any time. If `true`, allows the device to bypass the proxy server to display the login page for captive networks. If `true`, allows connecting directly to the destination if the proxy autoconfiguration (PAC) file is unreachable. The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy configuration. Starting in iOS 13 and macOS 10.15, only URLs that begin with `http://` or `https://` are allowed. This is only used if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`, and is ignored if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`. The password used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`. The user name used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`. The proxy server's port number. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores this if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`. The proxy server's network address. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`. The proxy type. For a manual proxy type, the profile contains the proxy server address, including its port, and optionally a user name and password. For an auto proxy type, you can enter a PAC URL. PEM-encoded cer

declaration.title: Global HTTP Proxy
declaration.description: The payload that configures a global HTTP proxy.

payload.content: PEM-encoded cer

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Type
payloadkeys.content: The proxy type. For a manual proxy type, the profile contains the proxy server address, including its port, and optionally a user name and password. For an auto proxy type, you can enter a PAC URL.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Server
payloadkeys.content: The proxy server's network address. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Server Port
payloadkeys.content: The proxy server's port number. The device requires this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores this if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Username
payloadkeys.content: The user name used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Password
payloadkeys.content: The password used to authenticate to the proxy server. The device only uses this if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`, and ignores it if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy PAC URL
payloadkeys.content: The URL of the PAC file that defines the proxy configuration. Starting in iOS 13 and macOS 10.15, only URLs that begin with `http://` or `https://` are allowed. This is only used if `ProxyType` is set to `Automatic`, and is ignored if `ProxyType` is set to `Manual`.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy PAC Fallback Allowed
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, allows connecting directly to the destination if the proxy autoconfiguration (PAC) file is unreachable.

payloadkeys.title: Proxy Bypass Allowed
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, allows the device to bypass the proxy server to display the login page for captive networks.

notes.content: There can only be one payload of this type on the device at any time.

items.title: Relay
items.summary: The payload that configures relay settings.
items.searchKeys: AllowDNSFailover UIToggleEnabled RelayUUID ExcludedFQDNs ExcludedFQDNsElement MatchFQDNs MatchFQDNsElement ExcludedDomains ExcludedDomainsElement MatchDomains MatchDomainsElement Relays Relay RawPublicKeys RawPublicKeysElement PayloadCertificateUUID AdditionalHTTPHeaderFields ANY HTTP2RelayURL HTTP3RelayURL
items.searchTitles: Relay Allow DNS Failover UI Toggle Enabled Excluded FQDNs Excluded FQDNs Element Match FQDNs Match FQDNs Element Excluded Domains Excluded Domains Element Match Domains Match Domains Element Relays Network Relay Raw Public Keys Raw Public Key Element Certificate UUID Additional HTTP Header Fields HTTP/2 Relay URL HTTP/3 Relay URL
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures relay settings. If `true`, the device allows the relay to failover to the default system DNS resolver. If `true`, the device allows the user to disable this network relay configuration. A globally unique identifier for this relay configuration. The system uses this UUID to route managed apps through the servers in `Relays`. This key is required for user enrollment. A list of Fully Qualified Domain Names (FQDNs) to exclude from routing through the servers contained in `Relays`. Any connection that matches an FQDN in the list exactly won't use the relay server. When `MatchDomains` is also present, any FQDN listed in the list should be a subdomain of at least one `MatchDomain` value, otherwise it will not have any effect. A list of Fully Qualified Domain Names (FQDNs) to be routed through the servers contained in `Relays`. Any connection that matches an FQDN in the list exactly uses the relay servers. If this list and `MatchDomains` are empty, the system routes traffic to all domains to the relay servers, except those that match an excluded domain or excluded FQDN. A list of domain strings to exclude from routing through the servers in `Relays`. Any connection that matches a domain in the list exactly or is a subdomain of the listed domain won't use the relay server. A list of domain strings that the system uses to determine which connection to route through the servers in `Relays`.

Any connection that matches a domain in the list exactly or is a subdomain of the listed domain uses the relay servers, unless it matches a domain in `ExcludedDomains`.

If this list and `MatchFQDNs` are empty, the system routes traffic to all domains to the relay servers, except those that match an excluded domain or excluded FQDN. An array of dictionaries that describe one or more relay servers that the system can chain together. An array of DER-encoded raw public keys that the system uses to authenticate the server during a TLS handshake. The server needs to use one of the keys in the handshake to authenticate.

If this array is empty, the system uses the default TLS trust evaluation. The UUID that points to an identity certificate payload, which the system uses to authenticate the user to the relay server. A dictionary that contains custom HTTP header keys and values to add to each request. The dictionary key name represents the HTTP header field name to use, and the dictionary value is the string to use as the HTTP header field value. The HTTP header field value for the corresponding header field name. The URL or URI template, as defined in RFC 9298, of a relay server that's reachable using HTTP/2 and supports proxying TCP and UDP using the CONNECT method.

Each relay needs to include either `HTTP2RelayURL` or `HTTP3RelayURL`, or it can include both. The URL or URI template, as defined in RFC 9298, of a relay server that's reachable using HTTP/3 and supports proxying TCP and UDP using the CONNECT method.

Each relay needs to include either `HTTP2RelayURL` or `HTTP3RelayURL`, or it can include both.

The URL or URI template, as defined in RFC 9298, of a relay server that's reachable using HTTP/3 and supports proxying TCP and UDP using the CONNECT method.

Each relay needs to include either `HTTP2RelayURL` or `HTTP3RelayURL`, or it can include both.

subkeys.title: HTTP/2 Relay URL
subkeys.content:
The URL or URI template, as defined in RFC 9298, of a relay server that's reachable using HTTP/2 and supports proxying TCP and UDP using the CONNECT method.

Each relay needs to include either `HTTP2RelayURL` or `HTTP3RelayURL`, or it can include both.

subkeys.title: Additional HTTP Header Fields
subkeys.content: A dictionary that contains custom HTTP header keys and values to add to each request. The dictionary key name represents the HTTP header field name to use, and the dictionary value is the string to use as the HTTP header field value.
The HTTP header field value for the corresponding header field name.

subkeys.title: Certificate UUID
subkeys.content: The UUID that points to an identity certificate payload, which the system uses to authenticate the user to the relay server.

subkeys.title: Raw Public Keys
subkeys.content:
An array of DER-encoded raw public keys that the system uses to authenticate the server during a TLS handshake. The server needs to use one of the keys in the handshake to authenticate.

If this array is empty, the system uses the default TLS trust evaluation.

payloadkeys.title: Match Domains
payloadkeys.content:
A list of domain strings that the system uses to determine which connection to route through the servers in `Relays`.

Any connection that matches a domain in the list exactly or is a subdomain of the listed domain uses the relay servers, unless it matches a domain in `ExcludedDomains`.

If this list and `MatchFQDNs` are empty, the system routes traffic to all domains to the relay servers, except those that match an excluded domain or excluded FQDN.

payloadkeys.title: Excluded Domains
payloadkeys.content: A list of domain strings to exclude from routing through the servers in `Relays`. Any connection that matches a domain in the list exactly or is a subdomain of the listed domain won't use the relay server.

payloadkeys.title: Match FQDNs
payloadkeys.content: A list of Fully Qualified Domain Names (FQDNs) to be routed through the servers contained in `Relays`. Any connection that matches an FQDN in the list exactly uses the relay servers. If this list and `MatchDomains` are empty, the system routes traffic to all domains to the relay servers, except those that match an excluded domain or excluded FQDN.

payloadkeys.title: Excluded FQDNs
payloadkeys.content: A list of Fully Qualified Domain Names (FQDNs) to exclude from routing through the servers contained in `Relays`. Any connection that matches an FQDN in the list exactly won't use the relay server. When `MatchDomains` is also present, any FQDN listed in the list should be a subdomain of at least one `MatchDomain` value, otherwise it will not have any effect.
A globally unique identifier for this relay configuration. The system uses this UUID to route managed apps through the servers in `Relays`. This key is required for user enrollment.

payloadkeys.title: UI Toggle Enabled
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the device allows the user to disable this network relay configuration.

payloadkeys.title: Allow DNS Failover
payloadkeys.content: If `true`, the device allows the relay to failover to the default system DNS resolver.

items.title: 802.1X: Second Active Ethernet
items.summary: The payload that configures the second wired, active Ethernet interface.
items.searchKeys: ANY
items.searchContent:
The payload that configures the second wired, active Ethernet interface. This payload's contents contain these profile-specific keys:

- Interface (String): This payload uses the value `SecondActiveEthernet`.
- EAPClientConfiguration (`EAPClientConfiguration`): The dictionary that defines the enterprise profile for the network.
- SetupModes (String): The type of connection mode, which is either "System" or "Loginwindow." "System" is the default.

Payloads with `active` in their name apply to Ethernet interfaces that are working at the time of profile installation. If there's no active Ethernet interface working, this payload configures the interface with the highest service-order priority. Keys relevant to 802.1x configuration.  User enrollment payloads do not support the various proxy keys including ProxyType, ProxyServer, ProxyServerPort, ProxyUsername, ProxyPassword,, ProxyPACURL and ProxyPACFallbackAllowed.
