Available in macOS 14 and later. If the value of this key is `app-proxy`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the application layer. If the value of this key is `packet-tunnel`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the IP layer.

Available in macOS 14 and later. If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required.

Available in macOS 14 and later. If the VPNSubType field contains the bundle identifier of an app that contains multiple VPN providers of the same type (app-proxy or packet-tunnel), then the system uses this field to choose which provider to use for this configuration. If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required.

Available in macOS 14 and later. The password to use for the account credentials. Only used if `AuthenticationMethod` is `Password`.

Available in macOS 14 and later. The UUID of the identity certificate as the account credential. If `AuthenticationMethod` is `Certificate`, and extended authentication (EAP) isn't used, this certificate is sent out for IKE client authentication. If extended authentication is used, this certificate can be used for EAP-TLS.

Available in macOS 14 and later. Determines when and how the system uses an OnDemand VPN.

Available in macOS 14 and later. A URL to probe. This rule matches when this URL is successfully fetched (returns a `200` HTTP status code) without redirection. Not available in watchOS. An array of SSIDs to match against the current network. If the network isn't a Wi-Fi network or if the SSID doesn't appear in this array, the match fails.
Omit this key and the corresponding array to match against any SSID. An interface type. If specified, this rule matches only if the primary network interface hardware matches the specified type. An array of IP addresses. This rule matches if any of the network's specified DNS servers match any entry in the array.
The system supports matching with a single wildcard. For example, `17.*` matches any DNS server in the `17.0.0.0/8` subnet. An array of domain names. This rule matches if any of the domain names in the specified list matches any domain in the device's search domains list.
The system supports a wildcard (`*`) prefix. For example, `*.example.com` matches against either `mydomain.example.com` or `yourdomain.example.com`. An array of dictionaries that provides rules similar to the `OnDemandRules` dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. This value is only for use with dictionaries in which the `Action` value is `EvaluateConnection`. The system evaluates these dictionaries in order and the first dictionary that matches determines the behavior. Not available in watchOS. A dictionary that provides rules similar to the OnDemandRules dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. These dictionaries are evaluated in order, and the behavior is determined by the first dictionary that matches.
The keys allowed in each dictionary are described below. Note: This array is used only for dictionaries in which EvaluateConnection is the Action value. An HTTP or HTTPS (preferred) URL to probe, using a GET request. If the URL's hostname can't be resolved, if the server is unreachable, or if the server doesn't respond with a 200 HTTP status code, a VPN connection is established in response.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'. An array of IP addresses of DNS servers to use for resolving the specified domains. These servers don't need to be part of the device's current network configuration. If these DNS servers aren't reachable, the system establishes a VPN connection. These DNS servers need to be either internal DNS servers or trusted external DNS servers.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'. Defines the VPN behavior for the specified domains. Allowed values are:
* 'ConnectIfNeeded': The specified domains should trigger a VPN connection attempt if domain name resolution fails, such as when the DNS server indicates that it can't resolve the domain, responds with a redirection to a different server, or fails to respond (timeout).
* 'NeverConnect': The specified domains should never trigger a VPN connection attempt. The domains to apply this evaluation. The action to take if this dictionary matches the current network. Possible values are:
- `Allow`: Deprecated. Allow VPN On Demand to connect if triggered.
- `Connect`: Unconditionally initiate a VPN connection on the next network attempt.
- `Disconnect`: Tear down the VPN connection and don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
- `EvaluateConnection`: Evaluate the ActionParameters array for each connection attempt.
- `Ignore`: Leave any existing VPN connection up, but don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
Only the `Disconnect` action is available on watchOS 10 and later. If `1`, the system brings up the VPN on demand.

Available in macOS 14 and later. If `1`, then all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally-defined routes. If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores the value of `EnforceRoutes`.

Available in macOS 14 and later. The number of seconds before the VPN disconnects. This value is only used if `DisconnectOnIdle` is `1`.

Available in macOS 14 and later. If `1`, the VPN disconnects automatically disconnect after a period defined by `DisconnectOnIdleTimer`.

Available in macOS 14 and later. The type of authentication method to use: `Password`, `Certificate`, or `Password+Certificate`.

Available in macOS 14 and later. The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `AlwaysOn`. Not available in tvOS or watchOS. The array of captive networking apps whose traffic is allowed outside the VPN tunnel, to perform captive network handling. Used only when `AllowAllCaptiveNetworkPlugins` is `false`. The bundle identifier for the app that's allowed on the captive network. If `1`, allows traffic from all captive networking apps outside the VPN tunnel to perform captive network handling. If `1`, allows traffic from Captive Web Sheet outside the VPN tunnel. An array that contains an arbitrary number of apps whose connections occur outside the VPN. Limit the exception to only the specified list of protocols, with support for `UDP` only. The app's bundle identifier. An array that contains an arbitrary number of service exceptions. The action to take with network connections from the named service. The name of a service that's exempt from Always On VPN.

`CellularServices` is available in iOS 11.3 and later; it exempts `VoLTE`, `IMS` and `MMS`. WiFiCalling is exempted in iOS 13.4 and later.

`DeviceCommunication` is available in iOS 17.4 and later; it exempts network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi. An array that contains an arbitrary number of tunnel configurations. The interfaces to apply this configuration to. The type of connection, which needs to be `IKEv2`. If `1`, allows the user to disable the VPN configuration. The dictionary to use to configure `Proxies` for use with `VPN`. The port number of the HTTPS proxy. This field is required if `HTTPSProxy` is specified. The host name of the HTTPS proxy. If `true`, enables proxy for HTTPS traffic. The password used for authentication. The user name used for authentication. The port number of the HTTP proxy. This field is required if `HTTPProxy` is specified. The host name of the HTTP proxy. If `1`, enables proxy for HTTP traffic. An array of domains that defines which hosts use proxy settings for hosts. The URL to the location of the proxy auto-configuration file. Used only when `ProxyAutoConfigEnable` is `true`. If `true`, enables proxy auto discovery. If `true`, enables automatic proxy configuration. A dictionary to use for all VPN types. That UUID that points to an identity certificate payload. The system uses this identity to authenticate the user to the DNS resolver. If `0`, append the domains in the `SupplementalMatchDomains` list to the resolver's list of search domains. The list of domain strings used to determine which DNS queries use the DNS resolver settings in `ServerAddresses`. The system uses this key to create a split DNS configuration where it resolves only hosts in certain domains using the tunnel's DNS resolver. The system uses the default resolver for hosts that aren't in one of the domains in this list.

If `SupplementalMatchDomains` contains the empty string it becomes the default domain.

Split-tunnel configurations can direct all DNS queries to the VPN DNS servers before the primary DNS servers. If the VPN tunnel becomes the network's default route, the servers listed in `ServerAddresses` become the default resolver and the system ignores the `SupplementalMatchDomains` list. The primary domain of the tunnel. The list of domain strings used to fully qualify single-label host names. The array of DNS server IP address strings. These IP addresses can be a mixture of IPv4 and IPv6 addresses. The hostname of a DNS-over-TLS server to validate the server certificate, as defined in RFC 7858. If `ServerAddresses` isn't specified, the system uses the hostname to determine the server addresses. This key is required if the `DNSProtocol` is `TLS`. The URI template of a DNS-over-HTTPS server, as defined in RFC 8484, which needs to use the `https://` scheme. The system uses the hostname or address in the URL to validate the server certificate. If `ServerAddresses` isn't specified, the system uses the hostname or address in the URL to determine the server addresses. This key is required if the `DNSProtocol` is `HTTPS`. The transport protocol to communicate with the DNS server. The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `IKEv2`. The `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters` dictionaries. The SA lifetime (rekey interval) in minutes. An array of strings representing postquantum key exchange methods the device uses during SA establishment and rekey. You can specify up to seven items, which correspond to ADDKE1 - ADDKE7 from RFC 9370. The Diffie-Hellman group.

For `AlwaysOn` VPN in iOS 14.2 and later, the minimum allowed value is `14`.

`1`, `2`, and `5` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26. The integrity algorithm.

`SHA1-96` and `SHA1-160` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26. The encryption algorithm.

In watchOS and tvOS, the default value is `AES-256-GCM`.
`DES` and `3DES` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26. These parameters apply to Child Security Association unless `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters` is specified. If set to `1`, the device doesn't allow DES, 3DES, and Diffie-Hellman groups less than 14. Also the device requires the encryption algorithm specified for the IKE SA to be at least as cryptographically strong as the algorithm specified for the child SA. The device rejects this profile payload if these requirements are not met. If set to `0`, the VPN doesn't establish a connection if the server does not support or doesn't allow post-quantum key exchanges. Thd device ignores this key if `PostQuantumKeyExchangeMethods` is not present in `IKESecurityAssociationParameters` or `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters`. If set to `1`, the VPN doesn't establish a connection if the server doesn't support RFC 8784 or doesn't accept the PPK identifier specified in `PPKIdentifier`. The device ignores this key if `PPK` and `PPKIdentifier` are not present. The identifier for the Post-quantum Pre-shared key (PPK) the device uses for this VPN. This key is is used with VPN servers that support RFC 8784. If this key is present `PPK` must also be present. The Post-quantum Pre-shared key (PPK) the device uses for this VPN. This key is is used with VPN servers that support RFC 8784. If this key is present `PPKIdentifier` must also be present. If set to `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is set to `1`, the device excludes network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi from the tunnel. If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system excludes network traffic for the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) from the tunnel. If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system excludes internet-routable network traffic for cellular services (VoLTE, Wi-Fi Calling, IMS, MMS, Visual Voicemail, etc.) from the tunnel. Note that some cellular carriers route cellular services traffic directly to the carrier network, bypassing the internet. Such cellular services traffic is always excluded from the tunnel. If `1` and either `IncludeAllNetworks` or `EnforceRoutes` are `1`, then the system routes local network traffic outside of the VPN. The default for this value is `0` on macOS and `1` on iOS. If `1`, all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally-defined routes. If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores `EnforceRoutes`. If `1`, then the system routes all network traffic through the VPN, with some controllable exclusions, such as `ExcludeLocalNetworks`, `ExcludeCellularServices`, and `ExcludeAPNs` properties. The system always excludes the following traffic from the tunnel:

- Traffic necessary for connecting and maintaining the device's network connection, such as DHCP.
- Traffic necessary for connecting to captive networks.
- Certain cellular services traffic that's not routable over the internet and is instead directly routed to the cellular network. See the `ExcludeCellularServices` field for more information.
- Network communication with a companion device such as a watchOS device. If the value of this key is `app-proxy`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the application layer. If the value of this key is `packet-tunnel`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the IP layer. The Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) specifies the maximum size in bytes of each packet that the system sends over the IKEv2 VPN interface. Available in iOS 14 and later, and macOS 11 and later. If `1`, the system enables a tunnel over cellular data to carry traffic that's eligible for Wi-Fi Assist and also requires VPN.

Enabling fallback requires that the server support multiple tunnels for a single user.

This field is available in iOS 13 and later, and tvOS 17 and later. Not available in watchOS. If `1`, the system performs a certificate revocation check for IKEv2 connections. This is a best-effort revocation check and server response timeouts won't cause it to fail. If `1`,  enables Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) for IKEv2 Connections. The NAT Keepalive interval for Always On VPN IKEv2 connections. This value controls the interval that the device sends keepalive offload packets. The minimum value is 20 seconds. If no key is specified, the default is 20 seconds over Wi-Fi and 110 seconds over a cellular interface. If `1`, enables NAT keepalive offload for Always On VPN IKEv2 connections. The device sends keepalive packets to maintain NAT mappings for IKEv2 connections that have a NAT on the path. It sends keepalive packets at regular intervals when the device is awake. If `NATKeepAliveOffloadEnable` is `1`, the system offloads keepalive packets to hardware while the device is asleep.

NAT keepalive offload has an impact on the battery life due to the extra workload during sleep. The default interval for the keepalive offload packets is 20 seconds over Wi-Fi and 110 seconds over Cellular interface. The default NAT keepalive works well on networks with small NAT mapping timeouts but imposes a potential battery impact. If a network has larger NAT mapping timeouts, larger keepalive intervals may be safely used to minimize battery impact. Modify the keepalive interval through the `NATKeepAliveInterval` key. Only used if `DisconnectOnIdle` is `1`. The number of seconds before the VPN disconnects. On watchOS, maximum allowed value is 15 seconds If `1`, the VPN disconnects automatically after a period defined by `DisconnectOnIdleTimer`. If `1`, the system disables IKEv2 redirect. If not set, the system redirects an IKEv2 connection when it receives a redirect request from the server. If `1`, the system disables MOBIKE. If `1`, negotiations should use IKEv2 Configuration Attribute `INTERNAL_IP4_SUBNET` and `INTERNAL_IP6_SUBNET`. The maximum TLS version to use with EAP-TLS authentication. The minimum TLS version to use with EAP-TLS authentication. The common name of the server certificate. The system uses this name to validate the certificate sent by the IKE server. If not set, the system uses the remote identifier to validate the certificate. Common Name of the server certificate issuer. If set, this field causes IKE to send a certificate request based on this certificate issuer to the server. This key is required if the `CertificateType` key is included and the `ExtendedAuthEnabled` key is `1`. One of the following:

- `None`: No keepalive.
- `Low`: Send keepalive every 30 minutes.
- `Medium`: Send keepalive every 10 minutes.
- `High`: Send keepalive every 1 minute.

Not available in watchOS. A list of rules that determine when and how to use an OnDemand VPN. If `1`, the system disables the Connect On Demand toggle in Settings for this configuration. If `1`, enables VPN up on demand. The password to use for authentication. The user name to use for authentication. If `1`, enables EAP-only authentication. If `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`, this value is used for IKE authentication. If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required. Available in macOS 10.15 and later, tvOS 17 and later, and watchOS 10 and later. If the VPNSubType field contains the bundle identifier of an app that contains multiple VPN providers of the same type (app-proxy or packet-tunnel), then the system uses this field to choose which provider to use for this configuration. If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required. The password to use for the account credentials. Only used if `AuthenticationMethod` is `Password`. The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use as the account credential. If the value of `AuthenticationMethod` is `Certificate`, the system sends this certificate out for IKEv2 machine authentication. If extended authentication (EAP) is used, the system sends this certificate out for EAP-TLS authentication. The type of `PayloadCertificateUUID` to use for IKEv2 machine authentication. If this key is included, the system requires a value for `ServerCertificateIssuerCommonName`. The type of authentication method for the VPN.

To enable EAP-only authentication, set this to `None` and `ExtendedAuthEnabled` to `1`. If this is `None` and the `ExtendedAuthEnabled` key isn't set, the authentication configuration defaults to `SharedSecret`. The remote identifier. Identifier of the IKEv2 client. The IP address or host name of the VPN server. The dictionary that contains IPSec settings. Not available in watchOS. The on-demand rules dictionary. Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, this field is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries. Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries. Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, if this key is present, the system treats associated domain names as though they're associated with the `OnDemandMatchDomainsOnRetry` key. This behavior can be overridden by `OnDemandRules`. If `1`, enables bringing the VPN connection up on demand. The length of time to wait before disconnecting an on-demand connection. If `1`, disconnect after an on-demand connection idles. If `true`, prompts for a PIN when connecting to Cisco IPSec VPNs. The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the account credentials.

Only use this with Cisco IPSec VPNs and if the `AuthenticationMethod` key is to `Certificate`. The shared secret for this VPN account.

Only use this with L2TP and Cisco IPSec VPNs and if the `AuthenticationMethod` key is to `SharedSecret`. Present only if `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`. The value is `KeyID`. The system uses this value for L2TP and Cisco IPSec VPNs. The name of the group. For hybrid authentication, the string needs to end with "hybrid".

Present only for Cisco IPSec if `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`. A string that either has the value "Prompt" or isn't present. If `1`, enables Xauth for Cisco IPSec VPNs. The VPN account password for Cisco IPSec. The user name for the VPN account for Cisco IPSec. The authentication method for L2TP and Cisco IPSec. The IP address or host name of the VPN server. The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `L2TP` or `PTPP`. Not available in watchOS. The length of time to wait before disconnecting an on demand connection If `1`, disconnects after an on demand connection idles. If `1`, enables encryption on the connection. This key is for use with PPTP networks. If `1` and `CCPEnabled` is also `1`, enables CCPMPPE40 encryption. If `1` and `CCPEnabled` is also `1`, enables CCPMPPE128 encryption. An array of authentication protocols. For use of RSA SecurID, this array should have one value, `EAP`. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks. An array of authentication plugins. For use of RSA SecurID, this array should only have one value: `EAP-RSA`. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks. The IP address or host name of VPN server. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks. If `1`, uses a token card such as an RSA SecurID card for connecting. This key is for use with L2TP networks. If `TokenCard` is `1`, use this password for authentication. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks. The VPN account user name. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks. The dictionary that contains IPv4 settings. Not available in watchOS. If `1`, the system sends all network traffic over VPN. Only applies to Cisco IPsec and L2TP VPN types. The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `VPN`. An array of dictionaries defining On Demand Rules. A list of domain names. If the host name ends with one of these domain names and a DNS query for that domain name fails, the system starts the VPN automatically.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS. A list of domain names. If the host name ends with one of these domain names, the system doesn't start the VPN automatically. The system uses this value to exclude a subdomain within an included domain.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS. A list of domain names. The system treats associated domain names as though they're associated with the `OnDemandMatchDomainsOnRetry` key. This behavior can be overridden by `OnDemandRules`.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS. If `1`, the Connect On Demand toggle in Settings is disabled for this configuration. Available in iOS 14 and later. Not available in watchOS. If `1`, enables VPN On Demand. If set to `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is set to `1`, the device excludes network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi from the tunnel. If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, then the system excludes the network traffic for the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) from the tunnel. Not available in watchOS. If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, then the system excludes internet-routable network traffic for cellular services (VoLTE, Wi-Fi Calling, IMS, MMS, Visual Voicemail, etc.) from the tunnel. Note that some cellular carriers route cellular services traffic directly to the carrier network, bypassing the internet. Such cellular services traffic is always excluded from the tunnel. Not available in watchOS. If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, routes all local network traffic outside the VPN. Not available in watchOS. If `1`, all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally defined routes.

If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores the value of `EnforceRoutes`.

Available in iOS 14.2 and later, and macOS 11 and later. Not available in watchOS. If `1``, routes all traffic through the VPN, with some exclusions. Several of the exclusions can be controlled with the `ExcludeLocalNetworks`, `ExcludeCellularServices`, `ExcludeAPNs` and `ExcludeDeviceCommunication` properties. The following traffic is always excluded from the tunnel:

- Traffic necessary for connecting and maintaining the device's network connection, such as DHCP.
- Traffic necessary for connecting to captive networks.
- Certain cellular services traffic that is not routable over the internet and is instead directly routed to the cellular network. See the ExcludeCellularServices property for more details.
- Network communication with a companion device such as a watchOS device.

Not available in watchOS. The type of VPN service. If the value is `app-proxy`, the service tunnels traffic at the app level. If the value is `packet-tunnel`, the service tunnels traffic at the IP layer. Not available in watchOS. The length of time to wait, in seconds, before disconnecting an on-demand connection. In watchOS, the maximum allowed value is `15`. If `1`, disconnects after an on-demand connection idles. If the VPN provider is implemented as a system extension, this field is required. Not available in watchOS. The bundle identifier for the VPN provider. Not available in watchOS. The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for account credentials. The authentication method to use. The IP address or hostname of the VPN server. The VPN account password. Only use this if `AuthenticationMethod` is set to `Password`. The VPN account username. The vendor-specific configuration dictionary, which the system reads only when `VPNSubType` has a value. Not available in watchOS. The login group or domain. Valid only for SonicWALL Mobile Connect. Not available in watchOS. The group to connect to on the head end. Valid for Cisco AnyConnect and Cisco Legacy AnyConnect. Not available in watchOS. The role to select when connecting to the server. Valid only for Juniper SSL and Pulse Secure. Not available in watchOS. The Kerberos realm name, which needs to be properly capitalized. Valid only for Juniper SSL and Pulse Secure. Not available in watchOS. The description of the VPN connection that the system displays on the device. Not available in watchOS. An identifier for a vendor-specified configuration dictionary when the value for `VPNType` is `VPN`.

If `VPNType` is `VPN`, the system requires this field. If the configuration targets a VPN solution that uses a VPN plugin, then this field contains the bundle identifier of the plugin. Here are some examples:

- Cisco AnyConnect: `com.cisco.anyconnect.applevpn.plugin`
- Juniper SSL: `net.juniper.sslvpn`
- F5 SSL: `com.f5.F5-Edge-Client.vpnplugin`
- SonicWALL Mobile Connect: `com.sonicwall.SonicWALL-SSLVPN.vpnplugin`
- ``Aruba VIA: `com.arubanetworks.aruba-via.vpnplugin`

If the configuration targets a VPN solution that uses a network extension provider, then this field contains the bundle identifier of the app that contains the provider. Contact the VPN solution vendor for the value of the identifier.

If `VPNType` is `IKEv2`, then the `VPNSubType` field is optional and reserved for future use. If it's specified, it needs to contain an empty string.

Not available in watchOS. The type of the VPN, which defines which settings are appropriate for this VPN payload.

If the type is `VPN` or `TransparentProxy`, then the system requires a value for `VPNSubType`.

`TransparentProxy` is only available in macOS. `L2TP` and `IPSec` aren't available in tvOS. `AlwaysOn` is only available on iOS and Apple Watch pairing isn't supported with `AlwaysOn`. For a previously paired Apple Watch, all phone-watch communications cease when `AlwaysOn` is enabled. Not available in watchOS.

declaration.title: VPN
declaration.description: The payload that configures a VPN.

payloadkeys.title: Type
payloadkeys.content:
The type of the VPN, which defines which settings are appropriate for this VPN payload.

If the type is `VPN` or `TransparentProxy`, then the system requires a value for `VPNSubType`.

`TransparentProxy` is only available in macOS. `L2TP` and `IPSec` aren't available in tvOS. `AlwaysOn` is only available on iOS and Apple Watch pairing isn't supported with `AlwaysOn`. For a previously paired Apple Watch, all phone-watch communications cease when `AlwaysOn` is enabled. Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: VPN Subtype
payloadkeys.content:
An identifier for a vendor-specified configuration dictionary when the value for `VPNType` is `VPN`.

If `VPNType` is `VPN`, the system requires this field. If the configuration targets a VPN solution that uses a VPN plugin, then this field contains the bundle identifier of the plugin. Here are some examples:

- Cisco AnyConnect: `com.cisco.anyconnect.applevpn.plugin`
- Juniper SSL: `net.juniper.sslvpn`
- F5 SSL: `com.f5.F5-Edge-Client.vpnplugin`
- SonicWALL Mobile Connect: `com.sonicwall.SonicWALL-SSLVPN.vpnplugin`
- ``Aruba VIA: `com.arubanetworks.aruba-via.vpnplugin`

If the configuration targets a VPN solution that uses a network extension provider, then this field contains the bundle identifier of the app that contains the provider. Contact the VPN solution vendor for the value of the identifier.

If `VPNType` is `IKEv2`, then the `VPNSubType` field is optional and reserved for future use. If it's specified, it needs to contain an empty string.

Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: User Defined Name
payloadkeys.content: The description of the VPN connection that the system displays on the device. Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: Vendor Configuration Dictionary
payloadkeys.content: The vendor-specific configuration dictionary, which the system reads only when `VPNSubType` has a value. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Realm
subkeys.content: The Kerberos realm name, which needs to be properly capitalized. Valid only for Juniper SSL and Pulse Secure. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Role
subkeys.content: The role to select when connecting to the server. Valid only for Juniper SSL and Pulse Secure. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Group
subkeys.content: The group to connect to on the head end. Valid for Cisco AnyConnect and Cisco Legacy AnyConnect. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Login Group or Domain
subkeys.content: The login group or domain. Valid only for SonicWALL Mobile Connect. Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: VPN
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `VPN`.

subkeys.title: Account Username
subkeys.content: The VPN account username.

subkeys.title: Account Password
subkeys.content: The VPN account password. Only use this if `AuthenticationMethod` is set to `Password`.

subkeys.title: RemoteAddress
subkeys.content: The IP address or hostname of the VPN server.

subkeys.title: Authentication Method
subkeys.content: The authentication method to use.

subkeys.title: Certificate UUID
subkeys.content: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for account credentials.

subkeys.title: Provider Bundle Identifier
subkeys.content: The bundle identifier for the VPN provider. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Provider Designated Requirement
subkeys.content: If the VPN provider is implemented as a system extension, this field is required. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Enable Disconnect on Idle
subkeys.content: If `1`, disconnects after an on-demand connection idles.

subkeys.title: Disconnect on Idle time
subkeys.content: The length of time to wait, in seconds, before disconnecting an on-demand connection. In watchOS, the maximum allowed value is `15`.

The type of VPN service. If the value is `app-proxy`, the service tunnels traffic at the app level. If the value is `packet-tunnel`, the service tunnels traffic at the IP layer. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Include All Networks
subkeys.content:
If `1``, routes all traffic through the VPN, with some exclusions. Several of the exclusions can be controlled with the `ExcludeLocalNetworks`, `ExcludeCellularServices`, `ExcludeAPNs` and `ExcludeDeviceCommunication` properties. The following traffic is always excluded from the tunnel:

- Traffic necessary for connecting and maintaining the device's network connection, such as DHCP.
- Traffic necessary for connecting to captive networks.
- Certain cellular services traffic that is not routable over the internet and is instead directly routed to the cellular network. See the ExcludeCellularServices property for more details.
- Network communication with a companion device such as a watchOS device.

Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Enforce Routes
subkeys.content:
If `1`, all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally defined routes.

If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores the value of `EnforceRoutes`.

Available in iOS 14.2 and later, and macOS 11 and later. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Exclude Local Networks
subkeys.content: If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, routes all local network traffic outside the VPN. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Exclude Cellular Services
subkeys.content: If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, then the system excludes internet-routable network traffic for cellular services (VoLTE, Wi-Fi Calling, IMS, MMS, Visual Voicemail, etc.) from the tunnel. Note that some cellular carriers route cellular services traffic directly to the carrier network, bypassing the internet. Such cellular services traffic is always excluded from the tunnel. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Exclude APNs
subkeys.content: If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, then the system excludes the network traffic for the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) from the tunnel. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Exclude Device Communication
subkeys.content: If set to `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is set to `1`, the device excludes network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi from the tunnel.

subkeys.title: Enable VPN On Demand
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables VPN On Demand.

subkeys.title: Prevent users from toggling VPN On Demand
subkeys.content: If `1`, the Connect On Demand toggle in Settings is disabled for this configuration. Available in iOS 14 and later. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains Always
subkeys.content:
A list of domain names. The system treats associated domain names as though they're associated with the `OnDemandMatchDomainsOnRetry` key. This behavior can be overridden by `OnDemandRules`.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains Never
subkeys.content:
A list of domain names. If the host name ends with one of these domain names, the system doesn't start the VPN automatically. The system uses this value to exclude a subdomain within an included domain.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains On Retry
subkeys.content:
A list of domain names. If the host name ends with one of these domain names and a DNS query for that domain name fails, the system starts the VPN automatically.

In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: On Demand Rules
subkeys.content: An array of dictionaries defining On Demand Rules.

subkeys.title: On Demand Action
subkeys.content:
The action to take if this dictionary matches the current network. Possible values are:
- `Allow`: Deprecated. Allow VPN On Demand to connect if triggered.
- `Connect`: Unconditionally initiate a VPN connection on the next network attempt.
- `Disconnect`: Tear down the VPN connection and don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
- `EvaluateConnection`: Evaluate the ActionParameters array for each connection attempt.
- `Ignore`: Leave any existing VPN connection up, but don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
Only the `Disconnect` action is available on watchOS 10 and later.

subkeys.title: Action Parameters
subkeys.content: An array of dictionaries that provides rules similar to the `OnDemandRules` dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. This value is only for use with dictionaries in which the `Action` value is `EvaluateConnection`. The system evaluates these dictionaries in order and the first dictionary that matches determines the behavior. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Action Parameter
subkeys.content:
A dictionary that provides rules similar to the OnDemandRules dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. These dictionaries are evaluated in order, and the behavior is determined by the first dictionary that matches.
The keys allowed in each dictionary are described below. Note: This array is used only for dictionaries in which EvaluateConnection is the Action value.

subkeys.title: Domains
subkeys.content: The domains to apply this evaluation.

subkeys.title: Domain Action
subkeys.content:
Defines the VPN behavior for the specified domains. Allowed values are:
* 'ConnectIfNeeded': The specified domains should trigger a VPN connection attempt if domain name resolution fails, such as when the DNS server indicates that it can't resolve the domain, responds with a redirection to a different server, or fails to respond (timeout).
* 'NeverConnect': The specified domains should never trigger a VPN connection attempt.

subkeys.title: Required DNS Servers
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses of DNS servers to use for resolving the specified domains. These servers don't need to be part of the device's current network configuration. If these DNS servers aren't reachable, the system establishes a VPN connection. These DNS servers need to be either internal DNS servers or trusted external DNS servers.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: Required URL String Probe
subkeys.content:
An HTTP or HTTPS (preferred) URL to probe, using a GET request. If the URL's hostname can't be resolved, if the server is unreachable, or if the server doesn't respond with a 200 HTTP status code, a VPN connection is established in response.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: DNS Domain Match
subkeys.content:
An array of domain names. This rule matches if any of the domain names in the specified list matches any domain in the device's search domains list.
The system supports a wildcard (`*`) prefix. For example, `*.example.com` matches against either `mydomain.example.com` or `yourdomain.example.com`.

subkeys.title: DNS Server Address Match
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses. This rule matches if any of the network's specified DNS servers match any entry in the array.
The system supports matching with a single wildcard. For example, `17.*` matches any DNS server in the `17.0.0.0/8` subnet.

subkeys.title: Interface Type Match
subkeys.content: An interface type. If specified, this rule matches only if the primary network interface hardware matches the specified type.

subkeys.title: SSID Match
subkeys.content:
An array of SSIDs to match against the current network. If the network isn't a Wi-Fi network or if the SSID doesn't appear in this array, the match fails.
Omit this key and the corresponding array to match against any SSID.

subkeys.title: URL String Probe
subkeys.content: A URL to probe. This rule matches when this URL is successfully fetched (returns a `200` HTTP status code) without redirection. Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: IPv4 Settings
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary that contains IPv4 settings. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Override Primary Connection
subkeys.content: If `1`, the system sends all network traffic over VPN. Only applies to Cisco IPsec and L2TP VPN types.

payloadkeys.title: PPP
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `L2TP` or `PTPP`. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Account Username
subkeys.content: The VPN account user name. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: Account Password
subkeys.content: If `TokenCard` is `1`, use this password for authentication. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: Use Token Card
subkeys.content: If `1`, uses a token card such as an RSA SecurID card for connecting. This key is for use with L2TP networks.

subkeys.title: Remote Address
subkeys.content: The IP address or host name of VPN server. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: EAP Plugins
subkeys.content: An array of authentication plugins. For use of RSA SecurID, this array should only have one value: `EAP-RSA`. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: Protocol
subkeys.content: An array of authentication protocols. For use of RSA SecurID, this array should have one value, `EAP`. This key is for use with L2TP and PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: Enable CCPMPPE40
subkeys.content: If `1` and `CCPEnabled` is also `1`, enables CCPMPPE128 encryption.

subkeys.title: Enable CCPMPPE128
subkeys.content: If `1` and `CCPEnabled` is also `1`, enables CCPMPPE40 encryption.

subkeys.title: Enable CCP
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables encryption on the connection. This key is for use with PPTP networks.

subkeys.title: Enable Disconnect on Idle
subkeys.content: If `1`, disconnects after an on demand connection idles.

subkeys.title: Disconnect on Idle time
subkeys.content: The length of time to wait before disconnecting an on demand connection

payloadkeys.title: IPSec Settings
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary that contains IPSec settings. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Remote Address
subkeys.content: The IP address or host name of the VPN server.

subkeys.title: Authentication Method
subkeys.content: The authentication method for L2TP and Cisco IPSec.

subkeys.title: Username
subkeys.content: The user name for the VPN account for Cisco IPSec.

subkeys.title: Password
subkeys.content: The VPN account password for Cisco IPSec.

subkeys.title: XAUTH Enabled
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables Xauth for Cisco IPSec VPNs.

subkeys.title: XAUTH Password Encryption
subkeys.content: A string that either has the value "Prompt" or isn't present.

subkeys.title: Local Identifier
subkeys.content:
The name of the group. For hybrid authentication, the string needs to end with "hybrid".

Present only for Cisco IPSec if `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`.

subkeys.title: Local Identifier Type
subkeys.content: Present only if `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`. The value is `KeyID`. The system uses this value for L2TP and Cisco IPSec VPNs.

subkeys.title: Shared Secret
subkeys.content:
The shared secret for this VPN account.

Only use this with L2TP and Cisco IPSec VPNs and if the `AuthenticationMethod` key is to `SharedSecret`.

subkeys.title: Certificate UUID
subkeys.content:
The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use for the account credentials.

Only use this with Cisco IPSec VPNs and if the `AuthenticationMethod` key is to `Certificate`.

subkeys.title: Prompt for PIN
subkeys.content: If `true`, prompts for a PIN when connecting to Cisco IPSec VPNs.

subkeys.title: Enable Disconnect on Idle
subkeys.content: If `1`, disconnect after an on-demand connection idles.

subkeys.title: Disconnect on Idle time
subkeys.content: The length of time to wait before disconnecting an on-demand connection.

subkeys.title: Enable VPN On Demand
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables bringing the VPN connection up on demand.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains Always
subkeys.content: Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, if this key is present, the system treats associated domain names as though they're associated with the `OnDemandMatchDomainsOnRetry` key. This behavior can be overridden by `OnDemandRules`.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains Never
subkeys.content: Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, this key is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

subkeys.title: On Demand Match Domains On Retry
subkeys.content: Deprecated. A list of domain names. In iOS 7 and later, this field is deprecated (but still supported) in favor of `EvaluateConnection` actions in the `OnDemandRules` dictionaries.

subkeys.title: On Demand Rules
subkeys.content: The on-demand rules dictionary.

subkeys.title: On Demand Action
subkeys.content:
The action to take if this dictionary matches the current network. Possible values are:
- `Allow`: Deprecated. Allow VPN On Demand to connect if triggered.
- `Connect`: Unconditionally initiate a VPN connection on the next network attempt.
- `Disconnect`: Tear down the VPN connection and don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
- `EvaluateConnection`: Evaluate the ActionParameters array for each connection attempt.
- `Ignore`: Leave any existing VPN connection up, but don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
Only the `Disconnect` action is available on watchOS 10 and later.

subkeys.title: Action Parameters
subkeys.content: An array of dictionaries that provides rules similar to the `OnDemandRules` dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. This value is only for use with dictionaries in which the `Action` value is `EvaluateConnection`. The system evaluates these dictionaries in order and the first dictionary that matches determines the behavior. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Action Parameter
subkeys.content:
A dictionary that provides rules similar to the OnDemandRules dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. These dictionaries are evaluated in order, and the behavior is determined by the first dictionary that matches.
The keys allowed in each dictionary are described below. Note: This array is used only for dictionaries in which EvaluateConnection is the Action value.

subkeys.title: Domains
subkeys.content: The domains to apply this evaluation.

subkeys.title: Domain Action
subkeys.content:
Defines the VPN behavior for the specified domains. Allowed values are:
* 'ConnectIfNeeded': The specified domains should trigger a VPN connection attempt if domain name resolution fails, such as when the DNS server indicates that it can't resolve the domain, responds with a redirection to a different server, or fails to respond (timeout).
* 'NeverConnect': The specified domains should never trigger a VPN connection attempt.

subkeys.title: Required DNS Servers
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses of DNS servers to use for resolving the specified domains. These servers don't need to be part of the device's current network configuration. If these DNS servers aren't reachable, the system establishes a VPN connection. These DNS servers need to be either internal DNS servers or trusted external DNS servers.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: Required URL String Probe
subkeys.content:
An HTTP or HTTPS (preferred) URL to probe, using a GET request. If the URL's hostname can't be resolved, if the server is unreachable, or if the server doesn't respond with a 200 HTTP status code, a VPN connection is established in response.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: DNS Domain Match
subkeys.content:
An array of domain names. This rule matches if any of the domain names in the specified list matches any domain in the device's search domains list.
The system supports a wildcard (`*`) prefix. For example, `*.example.com` matches against either `mydomain.example.com` or `yourdomain.example.com`.

subkeys.title: DNS Server Address Match
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses. This rule matches if any of the network's specified DNS servers match any entry in the array.
The system supports matching with a single wildcard. For example, `17.*` matches any DNS server in the `17.0.0.0/8` subnet.

subkeys.title: Interface Type Match
subkeys.content: An interface type. If specified, this rule matches only if the primary network interface hardware matches the specified type.

subkeys.title: SSID Match
subkeys.content:
An array of SSIDs to match against the current network. If the network isn't a Wi-Fi network or if the SSID doesn't appear in this array, the match fails.
Omit this key and the corresponding array to match against any SSID.

subkeys.title: URL String Probe
subkeys.content: A URL to probe. This rule matches when this URL is successfully fetched (returns a `200` HTTP status code) without redirection. Not available in watchOS.

payloadkeys.title: IKEv2
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `IKEv2`.

subkeys.title: RemoteAddress
subkeys.content: The IP address or host name of the VPN server.

subkeys.title: LocalIdentifier
subkeys.content: Identifier of the IKEv2 client.

subkeys.title: RemoteIdentifier
subkeys.content: The remote identifier.

subkeys.title: AuthenticationMethod
subkeys.content:
The type of authentication method for the VPN.

To enable EAP-only authentication, set this to `None` and `ExtendedAuthEnabled` to `1`. If this is `None` and the `ExtendedAuthEnabled` key isn't set, the authentication configuration defaults to `SharedSecret`.

subkeys.title: Certificate Type
subkeys.content: The type of `PayloadCertificateUUID` to use for IKEv2 machine authentication. If this key is included, the system requires a value for `ServerCertificateIssuerCommonName`.

subkeys.title: PayloadCertificateUUID
subkeys.content: The UUID of the certificate payload within the same profile to use as the account credential. If the value of `AuthenticationMethod` is `Certificate`, the system sends this certificate out for IKEv2 machine authentication. If extended authentication (EAP) is used, the system sends this certificate out for EAP-TLS authentication.

subkeys.title: Account Password
subkeys.content: The password to use for the account credentials. Only used if `AuthenticationMethod` is `Password`.

subkeys.title: Provider Bundle Identifier
subkeys.content: If the VPNSubType field contains the bundle identifier of an app that contains multiple VPN providers of the same type (app-proxy or packet-tunnel), then the system uses this field to choose which provider to use for this configuration. If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required.

subkeys.title: Provider Designated Requirement
subkeys.content: If the VPN provider is implemented as a System Extension, then this field is required. Available in macOS 10.15 and later, tvOS 17 and later, and watchOS 10 and later.

subkeys.title: SharedSecret
subkeys.content: If `AuthenticationMethod` is `SharedSecret`, this value is used for IKE authentication.

subkeys.title: ExtendedAuthEnabled
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables EAP-only authentication.

subkeys.title: AuthName
subkeys.content: The user name to use for authentication.

subkeys.title: AuthPassword
subkeys.content: The password to use for authentication.

subkeys.title: Enable VPN On Demand
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables VPN up on demand.

subkeys.title: Prevent users from toggling VPN On Demand
subkeys.content: If `1`, the system disables the Connect On Demand toggle in Settings for this configuration.

subkeys.title: On Demand Rules
subkeys.content: A list of rules that determine when and how to use an OnDemand VPN.

subkeys.title: On Demand Action
subkeys.content:
The action to take if this dictionary matches the current network. Possible values are:
- `Allow`: Deprecated. Allow VPN On Demand to connect if triggered.
- `Connect`: Unconditionally initiate a VPN connection on the next network attempt.
- `Disconnect`: Tear down the VPN connection and don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
- `EvaluateConnection`: Evaluate the ActionParameters array for each connection attempt.
- `Ignore`: Leave any existing VPN connection up, but don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
Only the `Disconnect` action is available on watchOS 10 and later.

subkeys.title: Action Parameters
subkeys.content: An array of dictionaries that provides rules similar to the `OnDemandRules` dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. This value is only for use with dictionaries in which the `Action` value is `EvaluateConnection`. The system evaluates these dictionaries in order and the first dictionary that matches determines the behavior. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Action Parameter
subkeys.content:
A dictionary that provides rules similar to the OnDemandRules dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. These dictionaries are evaluated in order, and the behavior is determined by the first dictionary that matches.
The keys allowed in each dictionary are described below. Note: This array is used only for dictionaries in which EvaluateConnection is the Action value.

subkeys.title: Domains
subkeys.content: The domains to apply this evaluation.

subkeys.title: Domain Action
subkeys.content:
Defines the VPN behavior for the specified domains. Allowed values are:
* 'ConnectIfNeeded': The specified domains should trigger a VPN connection attempt if domain name resolution fails, such as when the DNS server indicates that it can't resolve the domain, responds with a redirection to a different server, or fails to respond (timeout).
* 'NeverConnect': The specified domains should never trigger a VPN connection attempt.

subkeys.title: Required DNS Servers
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses of DNS servers to use for resolving the specified domains. These servers don't need to be part of the device's current network configuration. If these DNS servers aren't reachable, the system establishes a VPN connection. These DNS servers need to be either internal DNS servers or trusted external DNS servers.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: Required URL String Probe
subkeys.content:
An HTTP or HTTPS (preferred) URL to probe, using a GET request. If the URL's hostname can't be resolved, if the server is unreachable, or if the server doesn't respond with a 200 HTTP status code, a VPN connection is established in response.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: DNS Domain Match
subkeys.content:
An array of domain names. This rule matches if any of the domain names in the specified list matches any domain in the device's search domains list.
The system supports a wildcard (`*`) prefix. For example, `*.example.com` matches against either `mydomain.example.com` or `yourdomain.example.com`.

subkeys.title: DNS Server Address Match
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses. This rule matches if any of the network's specified DNS servers match any entry in the array.
The system supports matching with a single wildcard. For example, `17.*` matches any DNS server in the `17.0.0.0/8` subnet.

subkeys.title: Interface Type Match
subkeys.content: An interface type. If specified, this rule matches only if the primary network interface hardware matches the specified type.

subkeys.title: SSID Match
subkeys.content:
An array of SSIDs to match against the current network. If the network isn't a Wi-Fi network or if the SSID doesn't appear in this array, the match fails.
Omit this key and the corresponding array to match against any SSID.

subkeys.title: URL String Probe
subkeys.content: A URL to probe. This rule matches when this URL is successfully fetched (returns a `200` HTTP status code) without redirection. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Dead Peer Detection Rate
subkeys.content:
One of the following:

- `None`: No keepalive.
- `Low`: Send keepalive every 30 minutes.
- `Medium`: Send keepalive every 10 minutes.
- `High`: Send keepalive every 1 minute.

Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: ServerCertificateIssuerCommonName
subkeys.content: Common Name of the server certificate issuer. If set, this field causes IKE to send a certificate request based on this certificate issuer to the server. This key is required if the `CertificateType` key is included and the `ExtendedAuthEnabled` key is `1`.

subkeys.title: ServerCertificateCommonName
subkeys.content: The common name of the server certificate. The system uses this name to validate the certificate sent by the IKE server. If not set, the system uses the remote identifier to validate the certificate.

subkeys.title: TLS Minimum Version
subkeys.content: The minimum TLS version to use with EAP-TLS authentication.

subkeys.title: TLS Maximum Version
subkeys.content: The maximum TLS version to use with EAP-TLS authentication.

subkeys.title: Use IPv4 / IPv6 Internal Subnet Attributes
subkeys.content: If `1`, negotiations should use IKEv2 Configuration Attribute `INTERNAL_IP4_SUBNET` and `INTERNAL_IP6_SUBNET`.

subkeys.title: Disable Mobility and Multihoming
subkeys.content: If `1`, the system disables MOBIKE.

subkeys.title: Disable Redirect
subkeys.content: If `1`, the system disables IKEv2 redirect. If not set, the system redirects an IKEv2 connection when it receives a redirect request from the server.

subkeys.title: Enable Disconnect on Idle
subkeys.content: If `1`, the VPN disconnects automatically after a period defined by `DisconnectOnIdleTimer`.

subkeys.title: Disconnect on Idle time
subkeys.content: Only used if `DisconnectOnIdle` is `1`. The number of seconds before the VPN disconnects. On watchOS, maximum allowed value is 15 seconds

subkeys.title: NAT Keep Alive Offload Enable
subkeys.content:
If `1`, enables NAT keepalive offload for Always On VPN IKEv2 connections. The device sends keepalive packets to maintain NAT mappings for IKEv2 connections that have a NAT on the path. It sends keepalive packets at regular intervals when the device is awake. If `NATKeepAliveOffloadEnable` is `1`, the system offloads keepalive packets to hardware while the device is asleep.

NAT keepalive offload has an impact on the battery life due to the extra workload during sleep. The default interval for the keepalive offload packets is 20 seconds over Wi-Fi and 110 seconds over Cellular interface. The default NAT keepalive works well on networks with small NAT mapping timeouts but imposes a potential battery impact. If a network has larger NAT mapping timeouts, larger keepalive intervals may be safely used to minimize battery impact. Modify the keepalive interval through the `NATKeepAliveInterval` key.

subkeys.title: NAT Keepalive Interval
subkeys.content: The NAT Keepalive interval for Always On VPN IKEv2 connections. This value controls the interval that the device sends keepalive offload packets. The minimum value is 20 seconds. If no key is specified, the default is 20 seconds over Wi-Fi and 110 seconds over a cellular interface.

subkeys.title: Enable perfect forward secrecy
subkeys.content: If `1`,  enables Perfect Forward Secrecy (PFS) for IKEv2 Connections.

subkeys.title: Enable certificate revocation check
subkeys.content: If `1`, the system performs a certificate revocation check for IKEv2 connections. This is a best-effort revocation check and server response timeouts won't cause it to fail.

subkeys.title: Enable fallback
subkeys.content:
If `1`, the system enables a tunnel over cellular data to carry traffic that's eligible for Wi-Fi Assist and also requires VPN.

Enabling fallback requires that the server support multiple tunnels for a single user.

This field is available in iOS 13 and later, and tvOS 17 and later. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Maximum Transmission Unit
subkeys.content: The Maximum Transmission Unit (MTU) specifies the maximum size in bytes of each packet that the system sends over the IKEv2 VPN interface. Available in iOS 14 and later, and macOS 11 and later.
If the value of this key is `app-proxy`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the application layer. If the value of this key is `packet-tunnel`, the VPN service tunnels traffic at the IP layer.

subkeys.title: Include All Networks
subkeys.content:
If `1`, then the system routes all network traffic through the VPN, with some controllable exclusions, such as `ExcludeLocalNetworks`, `ExcludeCellularServices`, and `ExcludeAPNs` properties. The system always excludes the following traffic from the tunnel:

- Traffic necessary for connecting and maintaining the device's network connection, such as DHCP.
- Traffic necessary for connecting to captive networks.
- Certain cellular services traffic that's not routable over the internet and is instead directly routed to the cellular network. See the `ExcludeCellularServices` field for more information.
- Network communication with a companion device such as a watchOS device.

subkeys.title: Enforce Routes
subkeys.content: If `1`, all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally-defined routes. If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores `EnforceRoutes`.

subkeys.title: Exclude Local Networks
subkeys.content: If `1` and either `IncludeAllNetworks` or `EnforceRoutes` are `1`, then the system routes local network traffic outside of the VPN. The default for this value is `0` on macOS and `1` on iOS.

subkeys.title: Exclude Cellular Services
subkeys.content: If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system excludes internet-routable network traffic for cellular services (VoLTE, Wi-Fi Calling, IMS, MMS, Visual Voicemail, etc.) from the tunnel. Note that some cellular carriers route cellular services traffic directly to the carrier network, bypassing the internet. Such cellular services traffic is always excluded from the tunnel.

subkeys.title: Exclude APNs
subkeys.content: If `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system excludes network traffic for the Apple Push Notification service (APNs) from the tunnel.

subkeys.title: Exclude Device Communication
subkeys.content: If set to `1` and `IncludeAllNetworks` is set to `1`, the device excludes network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi from the tunnel.

subkeys.title: Post-quantum Pre-shared Key
subkeys.content: The Post-quantum Pre-shared key (PPK) the device uses for this VPN. This key is is used with VPN servers that support RFC 8784. If this key is present `PPKIdentifier` must also be present.

subkeys.title: Post-quantum Pre-shared Key Identifier
subkeys.content: The identifier for the Post-quantum Pre-shared key (PPK) the device uses for this VPN. This key is is used with VPN servers that support RFC 8784. If this key is present `PPK` must also be present.

subkeys.title: Post-quantum Pre-shared Key Mandatory
subkeys.content: If set to `1`, the VPN doesn't establish a connection if the server doesn't support RFC 8784 or doesn't accept the PPK identifier specified in `PPKIdentifier`. The device ignores this key if `PPK` and `PPKIdentifier` are not present.

subkeys.title: Allow Post-quantum Key Exchange Fallback
subkeys.content: If set to `0`, the VPN doesn't establish a connection if the server does not support or doesn't allow post-quantum key exchanges. Thd device ignores this key if `PostQuantumKeyExchangeMethods` is not present in `IKESecurityAssociationParameters` or `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters`.

subkeys.title: Enforce Strict Algorithm Selection
subkeys.content: If set to `1`, the device doesn't allow DES, 3DES, and Diffie-Hellman groups less than 14. Also the device requires the encryption algorithm specified for the IKE SA to be at least as cryptographically strong as the algorithm specified for the child SA. The device rejects this profile payload if these requirements are not met.

subkeys.title: IKESecurityAssociationParameters
subkeys.content: These parameters apply to Child Security Association unless `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters` is specified.

subkeys.title: EncryptionAlgorithm
subkeys.content:
The encryption algorithm.

In watchOS and tvOS, the default value is `AES-256-GCM`.
`DES` and `3DES` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: IntegrityAlgorithm
subkeys.content:
The integrity algorithm.

`SHA1-96` and `SHA1-160` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: DiffieHellmanGroup
subkeys.content:
The Diffie-Hellman group.

For `AlwaysOn` VPN in iOS 14.2 and later, the minimum allowed value is `14`.

`1`, `2`, and `5` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: Post-quantum Key Exchange Methods
subkeys.content: An array of strings representing postquantum key exchange methods the device uses during SA establishment and rekey. You can specify up to seven items, which correspond to ADDKE1 - ADDKE7 from RFC 9370.

subkeys.title: LifeTimeInMinutes
subkeys.content: The SA lifetime (rekey interval) in minutes.

subkeys.title: ChildSecurityAssociationParameters
subkeys.content: The `ChildSecurityAssociationParameters` dictionaries.

subkeys.title: EncryptionAlgorithm
subkeys.content:
The encryption algorithm.

In watchOS and tvOS, the default value is `AES-256-GCM`.
`DES` and `3DES` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: IntegrityAlgorithm
subkeys.content:
The integrity algorithm.

`SHA1-96` and `SHA1-160` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: DiffieHellmanGroup
subkeys.content:
The Diffie-Hellman group.

For `AlwaysOn` VPN in iOS 14.2 and later, the minimum allowed value is `14`.

`1`, `2`, and `5` are available only in iOS, macOS, and visionOS prior to iOS 26, macOS 26, and visionOS 26.

subkeys.title: Post-quantum Key Exchange Methods
subkeys.content: An array of strings representing postquantum key exchange methods the device uses during SA establishment and rekey. You can specify up to seven items, which correspond to ADDKE1 - ADDKE7 from RFC 9370.

subkeys.title: LifeTimeInMinutes
subkeys.content: The SA lifetime (rekey interval) in minutes.

payloadkeys.title: DNS
payloadkeys.content: A dictionary to use for all VPN types.

subkeys.title: DNS Protocol
subkeys.content: The transport protocol to communicate with the DNS server.

subkeys.title: Server URL
subkeys.content: The URI template of a DNS-over-HTTPS server, as defined in RFC 8484, which needs to use the `https://` scheme. The system uses the hostname or address in the URL to validate the server certificate. If `ServerAddresses` isn't specified, the system uses the hostname or address in the URL to determine the server addresses. This key is required if the `DNSProtocol` is `HTTPS`.

subkeys.title: Server Name
subkeys.content: The hostname of a DNS-over-TLS server to validate the server certificate, as defined in RFC 7858. If `ServerAddresses` isn't specified, the system uses the hostname to determine the server addresses. This key is required if the `DNSProtocol` is `TLS`.

subkeys.title: DNS Server Addresses
subkeys.content: The array of DNS server IP address strings. These IP addresses can be a mixture of IPv4 and IPv6 addresses.

subkeys.title: DNS Search Domains
subkeys.content: The list of domain strings used to fully qualify single-label host names.

subkeys.title: Domain Name
subkeys.content: The primary domain of the tunnel.

subkeys.title: Supplemental Match Domains
subkeys.content:
The list of domain strings used to determine which DNS queries use the DNS resolver settings in `ServerAddresses`. The system uses this key to create a split DNS configuration where it resolves only hosts in certain domains using the tunnel's DNS resolver. The system uses the default resolver for hosts that aren't in one of the domains in this list.

If `SupplementalMatchDomains` contains the empty string it becomes the default domain.

Split-tunnel configurations can direct all DNS queries to the VPN DNS servers before the primary DNS servers. If the VPN tunnel becomes the network's default route, the servers listed in `ServerAddresses` become the default resolver and the system ignores the `SupplementalMatchDomains` list.

subkeys.title: Supplemental Match Domains No Search
subkeys.content: If `0`, append the domains in the `SupplementalMatchDomains` list to the resolver's list of search domains.

subkeys.title: DNS Certificate UUID
subkeys.content: That UUID that points to an identity certificate payload. The system uses this identity to authenticate the user to the DNS resolver.

payloadkeys.title: Proxies
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use to configure `Proxies` for use with `VPN`.

subkeys.title: Proxy AutoConfig Enable
subkeys.content: If `true`, enables automatic proxy configuration.

subkeys.title: Proxy Auto Discovery Enable
subkeys.content: If `true`, enables proxy auto discovery.

subkeys.title: Proxy Server URL
subkeys.content: The URL to the location of the proxy auto-configuration file. Used only when `ProxyAutoConfigEnable` is `true`.

subkeys.title: Supplemental Match Domains
subkeys.content: An array of domains that defines which hosts use proxy settings for hosts.

subkeys.title: Enable HTTP
subkeys.content: If `1`, enables proxy for HTTP traffic.

subkeys.title: HTTP Proxy
subkeys.content: The host name of the HTTP proxy.

subkeys.title: HTTP Port
subkeys.content: The port number of the HTTP proxy. This field is required if `HTTPProxy` is specified.

subkeys.title: HTTP ProxyUsername
subkeys.content: The user name used for authentication.

subkeys.title: HTTP ProxyPassword
subkeys.content: The password used for authentication.

subkeys.title: Enable HTTPS
subkeys.content: If `true`, enables proxy for HTTPS traffic.

subkeys.title: HTTPS Proxy
subkeys.content: The host name of the HTTPS proxy.

subkeys.title: HTTPS Port
subkeys.content: The port number of the HTTPS proxy. This field is required if `HTTPSProxy` is specified.

payloadkeys.title: AlwaysOn
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `AlwaysOn`. Not available in tvOS or watchOS.

subkeys.title: UI Toggle Enabled
subkeys.content: If `1`, allows the user to disable the VPN configuration.

subkeys.title: TunnelConfigurations
subkeys.content: An array that contains an arbitrary number of tunnel configurations.

subkeys.title: Protocol Type
subkeys.content: The type of connection, which needs to be `IKEv2`.

subkeys.title: Interfaces
subkeys.content: The interfaces to apply this configuration to.

subkeys.title: ServiceExceptions
subkeys.content: An array that contains an arbitrary number of service exceptions.

subkeys.title: Service Name
subkeys.content:
The name of a service that's exempt from Always On VPN.

`CellularServices` is available in iOS 11.3 and later; it exempts `VoLTE`, `IMS` and `MMS`. WiFiCalling is exempted in iOS 13.4 and later.

`DeviceCommunication` is available in iOS 17.4 and later; it exempts network traffic used for communicating with devices connected via USB or Wi-Fi.

subkeys.title: Action
subkeys.content: The action to take with network connections from the named service.

subkeys.title: ApplicationExceptions
subkeys.content: An array that contains an arbitrary number of apps whose connections occur outside the VPN.

subkeys.title: Bundle Identifier
subkeys.content: The app's bundle identifier.

subkeys.title: LimitToProtocols
subkeys.content: Limit the exception to only the specified list of protocols, with support for `UDP` only.

subkeys.title: Allow Captive Web Sheet
subkeys.content: If `1`, allows traffic from Captive Web Sheet outside the VPN tunnel.

subkeys.title: Allow All Captive Network Plugins
subkeys.content: If `1`, allows traffic from all captive networking apps outside the VPN tunnel to perform captive network handling.

subkeys.title: AllowedCaptiveNetworkPlugins
subkeys.content: The array of captive networking apps whose traffic is allowed outside the VPN tunnel, to perform captive network handling. Used only when `AllowAllCaptiveNetworkPlugins` is `false`.

subkeys.title: Bundle Identifier
subkeys.content: The bundle identifier for the app that's allowed on the captive network.

payloadkeys.title: TransparentProxy
payloadkeys.content: The dictionary to use when `VPNType` is `TransparentProxy`. Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Authentication Method
subkeys.content:
The type of authentication method to use: `Password`, `Certificate`, or `Password+Certificate`.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Enable Disconnect on Idle
subkeys.content:
If `1`, the VPN disconnects automatically disconnect after a period defined by `DisconnectOnIdleTimer`.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Disconnect on Idle time
subkeys.content:
The number of seconds before the VPN disconnects. This value is only used if `DisconnectOnIdle` is `1`.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Enforce Routes
subkeys.content:
If `1`, then all the VPN's non-default routes take precedence over any locally-defined routes. If `IncludeAllNetworks` is `1`, the system ignores the value of `EnforceRoutes`.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Enable VPN On Demand
subkeys.content:
If `1`, the system brings up the VPN on demand.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: On Demand Rules
subkeys.content:
Determines when and how the system uses an OnDemand VPN.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: On Demand Action
subkeys.content:
The action to take if this dictionary matches the current network. Possible values are:
- `Allow`: Deprecated. Allow VPN On Demand to connect if triggered.
- `Connect`: Unconditionally initiate a VPN connection on the next network attempt.
- `Disconnect`: Tear down the VPN connection and don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
- `EvaluateConnection`: Evaluate the ActionParameters array for each connection attempt.
- `Ignore`: Leave any existing VPN connection up, but don't reconnect on demand as long as this dictionary matches.
Only the `Disconnect` action is available on watchOS 10 and later.

subkeys.title: Action Parameters
subkeys.content: An array of dictionaries that provides rules similar to the `OnDemandRules` dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. This value is only for use with dictionaries in which the `Action` value is `EvaluateConnection`. The system evaluates these dictionaries in order and the first dictionary that matches determines the behavior. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: Action Parameter
subkeys.content:
A dictionary that provides rules similar to the OnDemandRules dictionary, but evaluated on each connection instead of when the network changes. These dictionaries are evaluated in order, and the behavior is determined by the first dictionary that matches.
The keys allowed in each dictionary are described below. Note: This array is used only for dictionaries in which EvaluateConnection is the Action value.

subkeys.title: Domains
subkeys.content: The domains to apply this evaluation.

subkeys.title: Domain Action
subkeys.content:
Defines the VPN behavior for the specified domains. Allowed values are:
* 'ConnectIfNeeded': The specified domains should trigger a VPN connection attempt if domain name resolution fails, such as when the DNS server indicates that it can't resolve the domain, responds with a redirection to a different server, or fails to respond (timeout).
* 'NeverConnect': The specified domains should never trigger a VPN connection attempt.

subkeys.title: Required DNS Servers
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses of DNS servers to use for resolving the specified domains. These servers don't need to be part of the device's current network configuration. If these DNS servers aren't reachable, the system establishes a VPN connection. These DNS servers need to be either internal DNS servers or trusted external DNS servers.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: Required URL String Probe
subkeys.content:
An HTTP or HTTPS (preferred) URL to probe, using a GET request. If the URL's hostname can't be resolved, if the server is unreachable, or if the server doesn't respond with a 200 HTTP status code, a VPN connection is established in response.
This key is valid only if the value of 'DomainAction' is 'ConnectIfNeeded'.

subkeys.title: DNS Domain Match
subkeys.content:
An array of domain names. This rule matches if any of the domain names in the specified list matches any domain in the device's search domains list.
The system supports a wildcard (`*`) prefix. For example, `*.example.com` matches against either `mydomain.example.com` or `yourdomain.example.com`.

subkeys.title: DNS Server Address Match
subkeys.content:
An array of IP addresses. This rule matches if any of the network's specified DNS servers match any entry in the array.
The system supports matching with a single wildcard. For example, `17.*` matches any DNS server in the `17.0.0.0/8` subnet.

subkeys.title: Interface Type Match
subkeys.content: An interface type. If specified, this rule matches only if the primary network interface hardware matches the specified type.

subkeys.title: SSID Match
subkeys.content:
An array of SSIDs to match against the current network. If the network isn't a Wi-Fi network or if the SSID doesn't appear in this array, the match fails.
Omit this key and the corresponding array to match against any SSID.

subkeys.title: URL String Probe
subkeys.content: A URL to probe. This rule matches when this URL is successfully fetched (returns a `200` HTTP status code) without redirection. Not available in watchOS.

subkeys.title: PayloadCertificateUUID
subkeys.content:
The UUID of the identity certificate as the account credential. If `AuthenticationMethod` is `Certificate`, and extended authentication (EAP) isn't used, this certificate is sent out for IKE client authentication. If extended authentication is used, this certificate can be used for EAP-TLS.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Account Password
subkeys.content:
The password to use for the account credentials. Only used if `AuthenticationMethod` is `Password`.

Available in macOS 14 and later.

subkeys.title: Provider Bundle Identifier
subkeys.content:
If the VPNSubType field contains the bundle identifier of an app that contains multiple VPN providers of the same type (app-proxy or packet-tunnel), then the system uses this field to choose which provider to use for this configuration.
